500→. Network Isolation
   501→* All traffic flows through dedicated
   502→{at_sentry_container}
   503→* Each permitted traffic flow maintains exactly one controlled pathway
   504→* Precise ingress, forwarding, and NAT rules enforce strict boundaries
   505→* Only explicitly configured
   506→{st_port_map_s}
   507→allow traffic between
   508→{at_transit_network}
   509→and
   510→{at_enclave_network}
   511→*
   512→{at_bottle_container_s}
   513→have no direct network access; all connectivity flows through
   514→{at_sentry_container_s}
   515→* Comprehensive traffic logging
   516→
   517→. Service Separation
   518→* Each
   519→{at_bottle_service}
   520→fully isolated
   521→* No cross-service communication
   522→* Independent security policies
   523→* Clean state separation
   524→
   525→. Service Access Control
   526→* External access to services only permitted when
   527→{rbrn_entry_mode}
   528→is enabled
   529→* No inter-container communication allowed within pods
   530→* All service access must originate from outside the pod
   531→* Entry traffic flows: external -> pod ->
   532→{at_sentry_container}
   533→->
   534→{at_bottle_container}
   535→
   536→These properties hold across all operations and failure modes.
   537→
   538→== Getting Started
   539→
   540→For builders and operators new to
   541→{at_recipe_bottle},
   542→this section provides conceptual grounding and the setup journey.
   543→
   544→include::RBSGS-GettingStarted.adoc[leveloffset=+2]
   545→
   546→// ============================================================================
   547→// TIER 2: HOW
   548→// ============================================================================
   549→
   550→== Google API Engagement
   551→
   552→{at_recipe_bottle}
   553→invokes
   554→{gcp_service}
   555→APIs through
   556→{giam_service_account}
   557→JWT authentication and direct HTTPS REST calls.
   558→
   559→* *Authentication*:
   560→{giam_service_account_s}
   561→use private key credentials to sign JWTs with RS256.
   562→These JWTs are exchanged for
   563→{oauth_token_s}
   564→via Google's token endpoint.
   565→All API calls include the bearer token in the Authorization header.
   566→
   567→* *Invocation Pattern*:
   568→Operations execute through curl with JSON payloads and responses.
   569→Each call captures both HTTP status codes and response bodies to files for processing.
   570→Success is determined by HTTP 200/201/204 codes.
   571→
   572→* *Long-Running Operations*:
   573→{gcp_service}
   574→{gcp_lro_s}
   575→return operation IDs that must be polled until completion.
   576→The system polls at configurable intervals until completion.
   577→The system handles both success and error states.
   578→Operations utilize
   579→{rbbc_submit}
   580→and
   581→{rbbc_await}
   582→to handle all asynchronous operations.
   583→
   584→* *IAM Policies*:
   585→All IAM policy modifications enforce version 3 to ensure conditional role binding support across all
   586→{gcp_service}
   587→resources.
   588→
   589→== Operational Invariants
   590→
   591→=== Concurrent Session Safety
   592→
   593→{at_recipe_bottle}
   594→commands are designed for concurrent execution from multiple independent sessions against the same filesystem.
   595→This enables parallel workflows where different developers or automation systems invoke commands simultaneously.
   596→
   597→Concurrency safety is achieved through:
   598→
   599→* *Ephemeral Isolation*:
   600→Each command invocation creates a unique temporary directory using `BURD_NOW_STAMP` (timestamp + process ID + random component), ensuring intermediate files never collide.
   601→
   602→* *Unique Artifact Naming*:
   603→Cloud resources (source tarballs, build IDs) incorporate the invocation timestamp, preventing overwrite conflicts in
   604→{rbtgi_build_bucket}
   605→and
   606→{rbtgi_repository}.
   607→
   608→* *Read-Only Git Queries*:
   609→Git operations are non-mutating (status checks, metadata reads).
   610→The `git fetch` operation uses git's internal locking for safe concurrent execution.
   611→
   612→* *Semantic Preconditions*:
   613→Operations like
   614→{rbtgo_trigger_build}
   615→require clean git state.
   616→This is a workflow constraint ensuring reproducible builds, not a collision guard.
   617→
   618→NOTE: Concurrent builds of the same vessel produce independent images with different timestamps.
   619→Both builds succeed; neither overwrites the other.
   620→
   621→NOTE: The
   622→{gcb_service}
   623→default pool enforces a *Concurrent Build CPUs* quota per project per region (default: 10).
   624→If
   625→{rbrr_gcb_machine_type}
   626→consumes most of the CPU quota (e.g., `E2_HIGHCPU_8` uses 8 of 10 CPUs), a second
   627→{rbtgo_trigger_build}
   628→invocation will remain in `QUEUED` status while the first is `WORKING`.
   629→The
   630→{rbrr_gcb_min_concurrent_builds}
   631→regime variable declares the operator's minimum concurrency requirement.
   632→The nameplate audit (`rbw-nv`) and build preflight both query the Service Usage API to verify that `CPU_QUOTA ÷ vCPUs_per_build ≥`
   633→{rbrr_gcb_min_concurrent_builds}
   634→and hard-fail if quota is insufficient.
   635→See
   636→{rbtgo_quota_build}
   637→for the manual Console procedure to request a quota increase.
   638→

   868→[[mkc_interface_check]]
   869→// ⟦axl_voices axo_routine axe_bash_scripted⟧
   870→==== {mkc_interface_check}
   871→
   872→A utility that verifies network interface presence and configuration within a container context:
   873→
   874→[source,makefile]
   875→----
   876→# Waits until interface has an IPv4 address assigned, fails after timeout
   877→# @param 1 interface   Network interface name to check
   878→# @param 2 timeout_s   Number of seconds to wait before failing
   879→define check_interface
   880→timeout $(2)s sh -c "while ! ip addr show $(1) | grep -q \"inet \"; do sleep 0.2; done"
   881→endef
   882→----
   883→
   884→This utility generates precise validation commands for network interface readiness probes, accepting an interface name and timeout duration as parameters.

   886→// ============================================================================
   887→// TIER 3: REFERENCE
   888→// ============================================================================
   889→
   890→== Term Definitions
   891→
   892→=== Core {gcp_project_id} Terms
   893→
   894→[[at_recipe_bottle]]
   895→{at_recipe_bottle}::
   896→The comprehensive container management platform that enables secure, role-based building, deployment, and retrieval of containerized applications through
   897→{gcp_service}
   898→infrastructure.
   899→
   900→=== RB Role Infrastructure
   901→
   902→[[rbtr_role]]
   903→// ⟦axl_voices axo_identity⟧
   904→{rbtr_role}::
   905→An operational identity within the
   906→{at_recipe_bottle}
   907→system
   908→that an
   909→{at_operator}
   910→can assume to perform specific cloud operations.
   911→Each
   912→{rbtr_role}
   913→is implemented through a dedicated
   914→{giam_service_account}
   915→and corresponding
   916→{rbtgi_rbra_file}
   917→containing the necessary credentials, with the exception of the
   918→{rbtr_mason}
   919→which operates exclusively within cloud infrastructure.
   920→
   921→=== {giam_service_account} Definitions
   922→
   923→[[rbtr_payor]]
   924→// ⟦axl_voices axo_actor⟧
   925→{rbtr_payor}::
   926→An
   927→{rbtr_role}
   928→that enables an
   929→{at_operator}
   930→to manage billing accounts and create depot projects using OAuth user authentication rather than service accounts.
   931→The
   932→{rbtr_payor}
   933→operates through OAuth 2.0 desktop application flow, enabling individual developers to manage Recipe Bottle infrastructure without requiring organization-level permissions.
   934→
   935→[[rbtr_governor]]
   936→// ⟦axl_voices axo_actor⟧
   937→{rbtr_governor}::
   938→An
   939→{rbtr_role}
   940→that grants an
   941→{at_operator}
   942→{gcp_project_id}
   943→-scope Owner permissions without billing authority, designed for automation-friendly operations.
   944→
   945→[[rbtr_retriever]]
   946→// ⟦axl_voices axo_actor⟧
   947→{rbtr_retriever}::
   948→An
   949→{rbtr_role}
   950→that allows an
   951→{at_operator}
   952→read-only access to the
   953→{rbtgi_repository}
   954→for pulling
   955→{rbtgi_image_s}.
   956→
   957→[[rbtr_mason]]
   958→// ⟦axl_voices axo_actor⟧
   959→{rbtr_mason}::
   960→A cloud-native
   961→{giam_service_account}
   962→that operates exclusively within the
   963→{rbtgi_depot_project}
   964→to execute
   965→{at_recipe_bottle}
   966→build instructions from the
   967→{rbtr_director}.
   968→
   969→The
   970→{rbtr_mason}
   971→impersonates the implicit service agent for builds, replacing Google's auto-created service agent with deterministic permissions and predictable authorization.
   972→
   973→The
   974→{rbtr_mason}
   975→reads from the
   976→{rbtgi_build_bucket},
   977→carries out the build process within
   978→{gcb_service},
   979→and publishes
   980→{rbtgi_image_s}
   981→and
   982→{rbtgi_metadata_s}
   983→to the
   984→{rbtgi_repository}
   985→without requiring a local
   986→{rbtgi_rbra_file}
   987→since it operates entirely within the cloud infrastructure.
   988→
   989→[[rbtr_director]]
   990→// ⟦axl_voices axo_actor⟧
   991→{rbtr_director}::
   992→An
   993→{rbtr_role}
   994→that grants an
   995→{at_operator}
   996→full build lifecycle permissions including Cloud Build submission and
   997→{rbtgi_repository}
   998→management.
   999→

1→// ⟦axhrb_regime⟧
     2→{rbrn_regime}
     3→
     4→This regime specifies variables that define bottle service deployment configurations within the Recipe Bottle system.
     5→Each
     6→{rbrn_regime}
     7→instance represents a distinct service deployment with its own network, entry, and uplink settings.
     8→RBRN assignment files are found in `Tools/rbw/rbrn_*.env` and configure container deployment and network security.
     9→
    10→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
    11→{rbrn_moniker}
    12→
    13→Unique moniker for the bottle service instance.
    14→Must be 2-12 characters and form a valid cross-context identifier.
    15→
    16→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
    17→{rbrn_description}
    18→
    19→Human-readable description of the service purpose.
    20→Optional; 0-120 characters.
    21→
    22→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
    23→{rbrn_runtime}
    24→
    25→Container runtime for service deployment.
    26→Must be
    27→{rbrn_runtime_docker}
    28→or
    29→{rbrn_runtime_podman}.
    30→
    31→// ⟦axhrgb_group⟧
    32→{rbrn_group_ark_reference}
    33→
    34→Ark references identify which vessel consecration to deploy.
    35→At runtime, these resolve against the repository configuration to form complete image paths.
    36→
    37→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
    38→{rbrn_sentry_vessel}
    39→
    40→Vessel identifier for the sentry container image.
    41→
    42→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
    43→{rbrn_sentry_consecration}
    44→
    45→Consecration timestamp for the sentry container image.
    46→
    47→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
    48→{rbrn_bottle_vessel}
    49→
    50→Vessel identifier for the bottle container image.
    51→
    52→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
    53→{rbrn_bottle_consecration}
    54→
    55→Consecration timestamp for the bottle container image.
    56→
    57→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
    58→{rbrn_entry_mode}
    59→
    60→Selects entry service behavior for user-initiated connections.
    61→
    62→// ⟦axhrgb_group⟧
    63→{rbrn_group_entry}
    64→
    65→// ⟦axhrgc_gate⟧
    66→{rbrn_entry_mode}
    67→{rbrn_entry_mode_enabled}
    68→
    69→Variables in this group apply only when
    70→{rbrn_entry_mode}
    71→is
    72→{rbrn_entry_mode_enabled}.
    73→
    74→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
    75→{rbrn_entry_port_workstation}
    76→
    77→External port exposed on the transit network for user entry.
    78→Must be less than
    79→{rbrn_uplink_port_min}.
    80→
    81→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
    82→{rbrn_entry_port_enclave}
    83→
    84→Port used between sentry and bottle containers for entry traffic.
    85→Must be less than
    86→{rbrn_uplink_port_min}.
    87→
    88→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
    89→{rbrn_enclave_base_ip}
    90→
    91→Base IPv4 address that defines the enclave network range.
    92→
    93→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
    94→{rbrn_enclave_netmask}
    95→
    96→Network mask width for the enclave network addressing.
    97→Valid range: 8-30.
    98→
    99→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
   100→{rbrn_enclave_sentry_ip}
   101→
   102→IP address assigned to the sentry container for traffic mediation within the enclave.
   103→
   104→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
   105→{rbrn_enclave_bottle_ip}
   106→
   107→IP address assigned to the bottle container within the enclave.
   108→
   109→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
   110→{rbrn_uplink_port_min}
   111→
   112→Minimum port number for bottle-initiated outbound connections.
   113→Must be greater than any entry port.
   114→
   115→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
   116→{rbrn_uplink_dns_mode}
   117→
   118→Selects DNS resolution behavior for bottle-initiated requests.
   119→
   120→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
   121→{rbrn_uplink_access_mode}
   122→
   123→Selects IP access behavior for bottle-initiated connections.
   124→
   125→// ⟦axhrgb_group⟧
   126→{rbrn_group_access_allowlist}
   127→
   128→// ⟦axhrgc_gate⟧
   129→{rbrn_uplink_access_mode}
   130→{rbrn_uplink_access_mode_allowlist}
   131→
   132→Variables in this group apply only when
   133→{rbrn_uplink_access_mode}
   134→is
   135→{rbrn_uplink_access_mode_allowlist}.
   136→
   137→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
   138→{rbrn_uplink_allowed_cidrs}
   139→
   140→CIDR ranges for allowed bottle-initiated outbound traffic.
   141→
   142→// ⟦axhrgb_group⟧
   143→{rbrn_group_dns_allowlist}
   144→
   145→// ⟦axhrgc_gate⟧
   146→{rbrn_uplink_dns_mode}
   147→{rbrn_uplink_dns_mode_allowlist}
   148→
   149→Variables in this group apply only when
   150→{rbrn_uplink_dns_mode}
   151→is
   152→{rbrn_uplink_dns_mode_allowlist}.
   153→
   154→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
   155→{rbrn_uplink_allowed_domains}
   156→
   157→Domain names allowed for bottle-initiated DNS resolution.
   158→
   159→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
   160→{rbrn_volume_mounts}
   161→
   162→Podman volume mount arguments for the bottle container.
   163→Optional; 0-240 characters.
   164→
   165→// ⟦axhro_broach⟧
   166→{rbkro_broach}
   167→
   168→Sets defaults for all RBRN_ variables via `VAR="${VAR:-}"` pattern.
   169→Detects unexpected RBRN_ variables via compgen prefix scan.
   170→Builds ZRBRN_ROLLUP serialization string and ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV injection array.
   171→
   172→// ⟦axhro_validate⟧
   173→{rbkro_validate}
   174→
   175→Validates fields via buv_env_* type checkers.
   176→Validates RBRN_RUNTIME, RBRN_ENTRY_MODE, RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE, RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE enum constraints.
   177→Conditional validation: entry ports when ENTRY_MODE=enabled, allowlists when *_MODE=allowlist.
   178→
   179→// ⟦axhro_render⟧
   180→{rbkro_render}
   181→
   182→Renders via rbcr_* shared module with gate-aware section suppression.
   183→Sections: Core Service Identity, Container Image Configuration, Entry Service Configuration (gated), Enclave Network, Uplink Core, DNS Allowlist (gated), Access Allowlist (gated), Volume Mount.
   184→Displays unexpected variables in red.
   185→Validates after full render completes.
   186→

1→= Base Configuration Regime
     2→:doctype: book
     3→
     4→// tag::mapping-section[]
     5→//
     6→// Category declarations:
     7→// at_: Architectural terms
     8→// st_: Support/infrastructure terms
     9→//
    10→// Domain declaration: conf_<canonical_term>
    11→
    12→:at_bottle_container:        <<term_bottle_container,Bottle Container>>
    13→:at_sentry_container:        <<term_sentry_container,Sentry Container>>
    14→
    15→:at_bottle_service:          <<term_bottle_service,Bottle Service>>
    16→:at_bottle_service_s:        <<term_bottle_service,Bottle Services>>
    17→:at_enclave_network:         <<term_enclave_network,Enclave Network>>
    18→:at_enclave_network_s:       <<term_enclave_network,Enclave Networks>>
    19→:at_nameplate:               <<term_nameplate,Nameplate>>
    20→:at_nameplate_s:             <<term_nameplate,Nameplates>>
    21→:at_rbm_system:              <<term_rbm_system,RBM System>>
    22→:at_container_registry:      <<term_container_registry,Remote Container Registry>>
    23→:st_container:               <<term_container,Container>>
    24→:st_container_s:             <<term_container,Containers>>
    25→:st_subnet:                  <<term_subnet,Subnet>>
    26→:st_gateway:                 <<term_gateway,Gateway>>
    27→:st_published_image_s:       <<term_published_image,Published Images>>
    28→
    29→// end::mapping-section[]
    30→
    31→== Base Configuration
    32→
    33→=== Network Configuration
    34→
    35→.RBRR_DNS_SERVER
    36→[cols="1,4"]
    37→|===
    38→|Purpose |Specifies the upstream DNS resolver for all
    39→{at_bottle_service_s}.
    40→Each
    41→{at_sentry_container}
    42→uses this DNS server to resolve domain names.
    43→The
    44→{at_bottle_container}
    45→never directly accesses this: the
    46→{at_sentry_container}
    47→acts on its behalf when permitted.
    48→
    49→|Required |No
    50→
    51→|Format |Valid IPv4 address
    52→
    53→|Example |export RBRR_DNS_SERVER := 8.8.8.8
    54→|===
    55→
    56→=== System Configuration
    57→
    58→.RBRR_NAMEPLATE_PATH
    59→[cols="1,4"]
    60→|===
    61→|Purpose |Defines the directory containing all {at_nameplate} definitions
    62→
    63→|Required |No
    64→
    65→|Format |Relative path from {at_rbm_system} root
    66→
    67→|Default |"nameplates"
    68→
    69→|Example |export RBRR_NAMEPLATE_PATH := recipes/nameplates
    70→|===
    71→
    72→== Term Definitions
    73→
    74→[[term_bottle_service]]
    75→{at_bottle_service}::
    76→A complete service instance consisting of one bottle container and one sentry container, working together to provide secure functionality. Each service maintains strict file system and network boundaries while enabling controlled access to resources.
    77→
    78→[[term_enclave_network]]
    79→{at_enclave_network}::
    80→An isolated network connecting a bottle container to its sentry container.
    81→This network enforces strict security policies and provides the only communication path for the bottle container.
    82→
    83→[[term_nameplate]]
    84→{at_nameplate}::
    85→A configuration file stored in
    86→RBRR_NAMEPLATE_PATH
    87→that defines all aspects of a bottle service, including its images, network settings, and security policies.
    88→Each nameplate provides a complete service specification that the RBM System can instantiate.
    89→
    90→[[term_rbm_system]]
    91→{at_rbm_system}::
    92→The complete Recipe Bottle Makefile system, consisting of the
    93→RBM Console,
    94→RBM Config Makefile,
    95→nameplates,
    96→and
    97→associated tools.
    98→This system enables secure deployment and management of containerized services.
    99→
   100→[[term_container_registry]]
   101→{at_container_registry}:: 
   102→A service that stores and provides access to published images.
   103→
   104→[[term_container]]
   105→{st_container}:: 
   106→A lightweight, standalone executable package containing all necessary components to run a service.
   107→
   108→[[term_subnet]]
   109→{st_subnet}:: 
   110→A logically visible subdivision of IP network address space.
   111→
   112→[[term_gateway]]
   113→{st_gateway}:: 
   114→A network node that serves as the entry point between two networks.
   115→
   116→[[term_published_image]]
   117→{st_published_image}:: 
   118→An image that has been uploaded to a container registry.
   119→
   120→

1→// ⟦axhrb_regime⟧
     2→{rbrv_regime}
     3→
     4→This regime specifies variables that define container vessel configurations within the Recipe Bottle system.
     5→Each
     6→{rbrv_regime}
     7→instance represents a vessel image source, either bound from an external registry or conjured from a Dockerfile.
     8→RBRV assignment files are found in `rbev-vessels/*/rbrv.env` and configure image sourcing for bottle deployments.
     9→
    10→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
    11→{rbrv_sigil}
    12→
    13→Unique identifier for the vessel.
    14→Must be 1-64 characters forming a valid cross-context identifier.
    15→Must match the vessel directory name.
    16→
    17→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
    18→{rbrv_description}
    19→
    20→Human-readable description of the vessel purpose.
    21→Optional; 0-512 characters.
    22→
    23→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
    24→{rbrv_vessel_mode}
    25→
    26→Selects vessel provisioning strategy.
    27→Must be
    28→{rbrv_vessel_mode_bind}
    29→or
    30→{rbrv_vessel_mode_conjure}.
    31→
    32→// ⟦axhrgb_group⟧
    33→{rbrv_group_binding}
    34→
    35→// ⟦axhrgc_gate⟧
    36→{rbrv_vessel_mode}
    37→{rbrv_vessel_mode_bind}
    38→
    39→Variables in this group apply only when
    40→{rbrv_vessel_mode}
    41→is
    42→{rbrv_vessel_mode_bind}.
    43→Binding retrieves a pre-built image from an external registry without local build steps.
    44→
    45→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
    46→{rbrv_bind_image}
    47→
    48→Fully-qualified image reference to copy from an external registry.
    49→Must be a valid FQIN; 1-512 characters.
    50→
    51→// ⟦axhrgb_group⟧
    52→{rbrv_group_conjuring}
    53→
    54→// ⟦axhrgc_gate⟧
    55→{rbrv_vessel_mode}
    56→{rbrv_vessel_mode_conjure}
    57→
    58→Variables in this group apply only when
    59→{rbrv_vessel_mode}
    60→is
    61→{rbrv_vessel_mode_conjure}.
    62→Conjuring builds a vessel image locally from a Dockerfile and build context in the repository.
    63→
    64→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
    65→{rbrv_conjure_dockerfile}
    66→
    67→Path to Dockerfile relative to repository root.
    68→Must be 1-512 characters.
    69→
    70→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
    71→{rbrv_conjure_bldcontext}
    72→
    73→Build context path relative to repository root.
    74→Must be 1-512 characters.
    75→
    76→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
    77→{rbrv_conjure_platforms}
    78→
    79→Space-separated list of target platforms for multi-platform builds.
    80→Must be 1-512 characters.
    81→Examples: `linux/amd64`, `linux/amd64 linux/arm64`.
    82→
    83→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
    84→{rbrv_conjure_binfmt_policy}
    85→
    86→Controls whether cross-platform emulation via binfmt is permitted during builds.
    87→Must be
    88→{rbrv_binfmt_allow}
    89→or
    90→{rbrv_binfmt_forbid}.
    91→
    92→// ⟦axhro_broach⟧
    93→{rbkro_broach}
    94→
    95→Sets defaults for all RBRV_ variables via `VAR="${VAR:-}"` pattern.
    96→Detects unexpected RBRV_ variables via compgen prefix scan.
    97→Builds ZRBRV_ROLLUP serialization string.
    98→
    99→// ⟦axhro_validate⟧
   100→{rbkro_validate}
   101→
   102→Validates fields via buv_env_* type checkers.
   103→Checks RBRV_BIND_IMAGE or RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE presence invariant.
   104→Validates RBRV_CONJURE_BINFMT_POLICY enum constraint.
   105→
   106→// ⟦axhro_render⟧
   107→{rbkro_render}
   108→
   109→Renders via rbcr_* shared module.
   110→Sections: Core Vessel Identity, Binding Configuration, Conjuring Configuration.
   111→Displays unexpected variables in red.
   112→Validates after full render completes.
   113→

content.text: Excellent findings. I've discovered several important files. Now let me read the shell scripts, the legacy RBSRR file, the CRR file, the memos, and look for CLI scripts.

1→#!/bin/bash
     2→#
     3→# Copyright 2024 Scale Invariant, Inc.
     4→#

     6→# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
     7→# You may obtain a copy of the License at
     8→#
     9→#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
    10→#
    11→# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
    12→# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
    13→# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
    14→# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
    15→# limitations under the License.
    16→#
    17→# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
    18→#
    19→# Recipe Bottle Regime Nameplate - Validator Module
    20→
    21→set -euo pipefail
    22→
    23→# Multiple inclusion detection
    24→test -z "${ZRBRN_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbrn multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
    25→ZRBRN_SOURCED=1
    26→
    27→######################################################################
    28→# Internal Functions (zrbrn_*)
    29→
    30→zrbrn_kindle() {
    31→  test -z "${ZRBRN_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbrn already kindled"
    32→
    33→  # Set defaults for all fields (validate enforces required-ness)
    34→  RBRN_MONIKER="${RBRN_MONIKER:-}"
    35→  RBRN_DESCRIPTION="${RBRN_DESCRIPTION:-}"
    36→  RBRN_RUNTIME="${RBRN_RUNTIME:-}"
    37→  RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL="${RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL:-}"
    38→  RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL="${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL:-}"
    39→  RBRN_SENTRY_CONSECRATION="${RBRN_SENTRY_CONSECRATION:-}"
    40→  RBRN_BOTTLE_CONSECRATION="${RBRN_BOTTLE_CONSECRATION:-}"
    41→  RBRN_ENTRY_MODE="${RBRN_ENTRY_MODE:-}"
    42→  RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION="${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION:-}"
    43→  RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE="${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE:-}"
    44→  RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP="${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP:-}"
    45→  RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK="${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK:-}"
    46→  RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP="${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP:-}"
    47→  RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP="${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP:-}"
    48→  RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN="${RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN:-}"
    49→  RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE="${RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE:-}"
    50→  RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE="${RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE:-}"
    51→  RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS="${RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS:-}"
    52→  RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS="${RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS:-}"
    53→  RBRN_VOLUME_MOUNTS="${RBRN_VOLUME_MOUNTS:-}"
    54→
    55→  # Detect unexpected RBRN_ variables
    56→  local z_known="RBRN_MONIKER RBRN_DESCRIPTION RBRN_RUNTIME RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL RBRN_SENTRY_CONSECRATION RBRN_BOTTLE_CONSECRATION RBRN_ENTRY_MODE RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS RBRN_VOLUME_MOUNTS"
    57→  ZRBRN_UNEXPECTED=()
    58→  local z_var
    59→  for z_var in $(compgen -v RBRN_); do
    60→    case " ${z_known} " in
    61→      *" ${z_var} "*) : ;;
    62→      *) ZRBRN_UNEXPECTED+=("${z_var}") ;;
    63→    esac
    64→  done
    65→
    66→  # Build rollup of all RBRN_ variables for passing to scripts/containers
    67→  ZRBRN_ROLLUP=""
    68→  ZRBRN_ROLLUP+="RBRN_MONIKER='${RBRN_MONIKER}' "
    69→  ZRBRN_ROLLUP+="RBRN_DESCRIPTION='${RBRN_DESCRIPTION}' "
    70→  ZRBRN_ROLLUP+="RBRN_RUNTIME='${RBRN_RUNTIME}' "
    71→  ZRBRN_ROLLUP+="RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL='${RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL}' "
    72→  ZRBRN_ROLLUP+="RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL='${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL}' "
    73→  ZRBRN_ROLLUP+="RBRN_SENTRY_CONSECRATION='${RBRN_SENTRY_CONSECRATION}' "
    74→  ZRBRN_ROLLUP+="RBRN_BOTTLE_CONSECRATION='${RBRN_BOTTLE_CONSECRATION}' "
    75→  ZRBRN_ROLLUP+="RBRN_ENTRY_MODE='${RBRN_ENTRY_MODE}' "
    76→  ZRBRN_ROLLUP+="RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION='${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION}' "
    77→  ZRBRN_ROLLUP+="RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE='${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE}' "
    78→  ZRBRN_ROLLUP+="RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP='${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP}' "
    79→  ZRBRN_ROLLUP+="RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK='${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK}' "
    80→  ZRBRN_ROLLUP+="RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP='${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}' "
    81→  ZRBRN_ROLLUP+="RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP='${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP}' "
    82→  ZRBRN_ROLLUP+="RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN='${RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN}' "
    83→  ZRBRN_ROLLUP+="RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE='${RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE}' "
    84→  ZRBRN_ROLLUP+="RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE='${RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE}' "
    85→  ZRBRN_ROLLUP+="RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS='${RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS}' "
    86→  ZRBRN_ROLLUP+="RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS='${RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS}' "
    87→  ZRBRN_ROLLUP+="RBRN_VOLUME_MOUNTS='${RBRN_VOLUME_MOUNTS}'"
    88→
    89→  # Build docker env args array for container injection
    90→  # Usage: docker run "${ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV[@]}" ...
    91→  ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV=()
    92→  ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV+=("-e" "RBRN_MONIKER=${RBRN_MONIKER}")
    93→  ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV+=("-e" "RBRN_DESCRIPTION=${RBRN_DESCRIPTION}")
    94→  ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV+=("-e" "RBRN_RUNTIME=${RBRN_RUNTIME}")
    95→  ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV+=("-e" "RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL=${RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL}")
    96→  ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV+=("-e" "RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL=${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL}")
    97→  ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV+=("-e" "RBRN_SENTRY_CONSECRATION=${RBRN_SENTRY_CONSECRATION}")
    98→  ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV+=("-e" "RBRN_BOTTLE_CONSECRATION=${RBRN_BOTTLE_CONSECRATION}")
    99→  ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV+=("-e" "RBRN_ENTRY_MODE=${RBRN_ENTRY_MODE}")
   100→  ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV+=("-e" "RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION=${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION}")
   101→  ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV+=("-e" "RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE=${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE}")
   102→  ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV+=("-e" "RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP=${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP}")
   103→  ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV+=("-e" "RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK=${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK}")
   104→  ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV+=("-e" "RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP=${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}")
   105→  ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV+=("-e" "RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP=${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP}")
   106→  ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV+=("-e" "RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN=${RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN}")
   107→  ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV+=("-e" "RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE=${RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE}")
   108→  ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV+=("-e" "RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE=${RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE}")
   109→  ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV+=("-e" "RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS=${RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS}")
   110→  ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV+=("-e" "RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS=${RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS}")
   111→  ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV+=("-e" "RBRN_VOLUME_MOUNTS=${RBRN_VOLUME_MOUNTS}")
   112→
   113→  ZRBRN_KINDLED=1
   114→}
   115→
   116→zrbrn_sentinel() {
   117→  test "${ZRBRN_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbrn not kindled - call zrbrn_kindle first"
   118→}
   119→
   120→# Validate RBRN variables via buv_env_* (dies on first error)
   121→# Prerequisite: kindle must have been called; buv_validation.sh must be sourced
   122→zrbrn_validate_fields() {
   123→  zrbrn_sentinel
   124→
   125→  # Die on unexpected variables
   126→  if test ${#ZRBRN_UNEXPECTED[@]} -gt 0; then
   127→    buc_die "Unexpected RBRN_ variables: ${ZRBRN_UNEXPECTED[*]}"
   128→  fi
   129→
   130→  # Core nameplate identification
   131→  buv_env_xname       RBRN_MONIKER                 2     12
   132→  buv_env_string      RBRN_DESCRIPTION             0    120
   133→  buv_env_string      RBRN_RUNTIME                 1     16
   134→
   135→  # Validate runtime is docker or podman
   136→  case "${RBRN_RUNTIME}" in
   137→    docker|podman) : ;;
   138→    *) buc_die "Invalid RBRN_RUNTIME: '${RBRN_RUNTIME}' (must be 'docker' or 'podman')" ;;
   139→  esac
   140→
   141→  # Container image configuration
   142→  buv_env_fqin        RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL        1    128
   143→  buv_env_fqin        RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL        1    128
   144→  buv_env_fqin        RBRN_SENTRY_CONSECRATION        1    128
   145→  buv_env_fqin        RBRN_BOTTLE_CONSECRATION        1    128
   146→
   147→  # Entry point configuration
   148→  case "${RBRN_ENTRY_MODE}" in
   149→    disabled|enabled) : ;;
   150→    *) buc_die "Invalid RBRN_ENTRY_MODE: '${RBRN_ENTRY_MODE}' (must be 'disabled' or 'enabled')" ;;
   151→  esac
   152→
   153→  # Enclave network configuration
   154→  buv_env_ipv4        RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP
   155→  buv_env_decimal     RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK         8     30
   156→  buv_env_ipv4        RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP
   157→  buv_env_ipv4        RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP
   158→
   159→  # Verify IPs fall within declared subnet
   160→  zrbrn_ip_in_subnet RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}" "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP}" "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK}"
   161→  zrbrn_ip_in_subnet RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP}" "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP}" "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK}"
   162→
   163→  # Uplink configuration
   164→  buv_env_port        RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN
   165→  case "${RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE}" in
   166→    disabled|global|allowlist) : ;;
   167→    *) buc_die "Invalid RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE: '${RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE}' (must be 'disabled', 'global', or 'allowlist')" ;;
   168→  esac
   169→  case "${RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE}" in
   170→    disabled|global|allowlist) : ;;
   171→    *) buc_die "Invalid RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE: '${RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE}' (must be 'disabled', 'global', or 'allowlist')" ;;
   172→  esac
   173→
   174→  # Conditional entry port validation (Required: When ENTRY_MODE=enabled)
   175→  if [[ $RBRN_ENTRY_MODE == enabled ]]; then
   176→    buv_env_port    RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION
   177→    buv_env_port    RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE
   178→
   179→    test ${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION} -lt ${RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN} || \
   180→      buc_die "RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION must be less than RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN"
   181→    test ${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE} -lt ${RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN} || \
   182→      buc_die "RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE must be less than RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN"
   183→  fi
   184→
   185→  # Conditional allowlist validation (Required: When *_MODE=allowlist)
   186→  if [[ ${RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE} == allowlist ]]; then
   187→    buv_env_list_cidr RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS
   188→  fi
   189→  if [[ ${RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE} == allowlist ]]; then
   190→    buv_env_list_domain RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS
   191→  fi
   192→
   193→  # Volume mount configuration (Required: No)
   194→  buv_env_string      RBRN_VOLUME_MOUNTS           0    240
   195→}
   196→
   197→######################################################################
   198→# Public Functions (rbrn_*)
   199→
   200→# Load nameplate regime by moniker
   201→# Usage: rbrn_load_moniker <moniker>
   202→# Constructs path, verifies file exists, sources, kindles, and validates
   203→rbrn_load_moniker() {
   204→  local z_moniker="${1:-}"
   205→  test -n "${z_moniker}" || buc_die "rbrn_load_moniker: moniker argument required"
   206→
   207→  local z_nameplate_file="${RBCC_KIT_DIR}/${RBCC_RBRN_PREFIX}${z_moniker}${RBCC_RBRN_EXT}"
   208→  test -f "${z_nameplate_file}" || buc_die "Nameplate not found: ${z_nameplate_file}"
   209→
   210→  source "${z_nameplate_file}" || buc_die "Failed to source nameplate: ${z_nameplate_file}"
   211→  zrbrn_kindle
   212→  zrbrn_validate_fields
   213→}
   214→
   215→# Load nameplate regime by file path
   216→# Usage: rbrn_load_file <path>
   217→# Takes explicit file path, sources, kindles, and validates
   218→rbrn_load_file() {
   219→  local z_file="${1:-}"
   220→  test -n "${z_file}" || buc_die "rbrn_load_file: file argument required"
   221→  test -f "${z_file}" || buc_die "rbrn_load_file: file not found: ${z_file}"
   222→
   223→  source "${z_file}" || buc_die "Failed to source nameplate: ${z_file}"
   224→  zrbrn_kindle
   225→  zrbrn_validate_fields
   226→}
   227→
   228→# List available nameplate monikers
   229→# Usage: rbrn_list
   230→# Returns list of concrete nameplate monikers by globbing rbrn_*.env files
   231→rbrn_list() {
   232→  local z_nameplate_files=("${RBCC_KIT_DIR}/${RBCC_RBRN_PREFIX}"*"${RBCC_RBRN_EXT}")
   233→
   234→  for z_file in "${z_nameplate_files[@]}"; do
   235→    test -f "${z_file}" || continue
   236→    local z_basename="${z_file##*/}"
   237→    local z_moniker="${z_basename#${RBCC_RBRN_PREFIX}}"
   238→    z_moniker="${z_moniker%${RBCC_RBRN_EXT}}"
   239→    echo "${z_moniker}"
   240→  done
   241→}
   242→
   243→######################################################################
   244→# Cross-Nameplate Functions
   245→#
   246→# These iterate all nameplates by direct-sourcing .env files in
   247→# subshells to avoid kindle-once guard conflicts.
   248→#
   249→# rbrn_preflight: Requires RBCC kindled
   250→# rbrn_survey:    Requires RBCC, RBGC, RBGD kindled + RBRR loaded
   251→# rbrn_audit:     Requires RBCC, RBGC, RBGD kindled + RBRR loaded
   252→
   253→# Convert dotted-quad IPv4 to integer for subnet arithmetic
   254→zrbrn_ip_to_int() {
   255→  local z_a z_b z_c z_d
   256→  IFS='.' read -r z_a z_b z_c z_d <<< "$1"
   257→  echo $(( (z_a << 24) + (z_b << 16) + (z_c << 8) + z_d ))
   258→}
   259→
   260→# Validate that an IP falls within a subnet (dies if not)
   261→# Usage: zrbrn_ip_in_subnet LABEL IP BASE MASK
   262→zrbrn_ip_in_subnet() {
   263→  local z_label="$1" z_ip="$2" z_base="$3" z_mask="$4"
   264→  local z_ip_int=$(zrbrn_ip_to_int "${z_ip}")
   265→  local z_base_int=$(zrbrn_ip_to_int "${z_base}")
   266→  local z_net_mask=$(( (0xFFFFFFFF << (32 - z_mask)) & 0xFFFFFFFF ))
   267→  if [[ $(( z_ip_int & z_net_mask )) -ne $(( z_base_int & z_net_mask )) ]]; then
   268→    buc_die "${z_label}=${z_ip} is not within subnet ${z_base}/${z_mask}"
   269→  fi
   270→}
   271→
   272→# Cross-nameplate conflict validation (silent on success, dies on conflict)
   273→# Checks: port uniqueness, subnet non-overlap, enclave IP uniqueness
   274→rbrn_preflight() {
   275→  zrbcc_sentinel
   276→
   277→  # Collect structured data from all nameplates via subshell sourcing
   278→  local z_data
   279→  z_data=$(
   280→    for z_m in $(rbrn_list); do
   281→      z_f="${RBCC_KIT_DIR}/${RBCC_RBRN_PREFIX}${z_m}${RBCC_RBRN_EXT}"
   282→      (
   283→        source "${z_f}"
   284→        echo "${RBRN_MONIKER}|${RBRN_ENTRY_MODE}|${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION:-0}|${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE:-0}|${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP}|${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK}|${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}|${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP}"
   285→      )
   286→    done
   287→  )
   288→
   289→  # Parallel arrays for conflict detection (bash 3.2 compatible)
   290→  local z_ws_port_keys=()
   291→  local z_ws_port_vals=()
   292→  local z_enc_port_keys=()
   293→  local z_enc_port_vals=()
   294→  local z_ip_keys=()
   295→  local z_ip_vals=()
   296→  local z_net_starts=()
   297→  local z_net_ends=()
   298→  local z_net_owners=()
   299→
   300→  local z_mon z_entry z_ws z_enc z_base z_mask z_sentry z_bottle
   301→  while IFS='|' read -r z_mon z_entry z_ws z_enc z_base z_mask z_sentry z_bottle; do
   302→    test -n "${z_mon}" || continue
   303→
   304→    # Workstation and enclave port uniqueness (enabled entries only)
   305→    if [[ "${z_entry}" == "enabled" ]]; then
   306→      local z_i
   307→      for z_i in "${!z_ws_port_keys[@]}"; do
   308→        if [[ "${z_ws_port_keys[$z_i]}" == "${z_ws}" ]]; then
   309→          buc_die "Port conflict: RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION=${z_ws} claimed by both ${z_ws_port_vals[$z_i]} and ${z_mon}"
   310→        fi
   311→      done
   312→      z_ws_port_keys+=("${z_ws}")
   313→      z_ws_port_vals+=("${z_mon}")
   314→
   315→      for z_i in "${!z_enc_port_keys[@]}"; do
   316→        if [[ "${z_enc_port_keys[$z_i]}" == "${z_enc}" ]]; then
   317→          buc_die "Port conflict: RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE=${z_enc} claimed by both ${z_enc_port_vals[$z_i]} and ${z_mon}"
   318→        fi
   319→      done
   320→      z_enc_port_keys+=("${z_enc}")
   321→      z_enc_port_vals+=("${z_mon}")
   322→    fi
   323→
   324→    # Enclave IP uniqueness (all sentry and bottle IPs across nameplates)
   325→    local z_j
   326→    for z_j in "${!z_ip_keys[@]}"; do
   327→      if [[ "${z_ip_keys[$z_j]}" == "${z_sentry}" ]]; then
   328→        buc_die "IP conflict: ${z_sentry} claimed by ${z_mon} (sentry) and ${z_ip_vals[$z_j]}"
   329→      fi
   330→    done
   331→    z_ip_keys+=("${z_sentry}")
   332→    z_ip_vals+=("${z_mon}:sentry")
   333→
   334→    for z_j in "${!z_ip_keys[@]}"; do
   335→      if [[ "${z_ip_keys[$z_j]}" == "${z_bottle}" ]]; then
   336→        buc_die "IP conflict: ${z_bottle} claimed by ${z_mon} (bottle) and ${z_ip_vals[$z_j]}"
   337→      fi
   338→    done
   339→    z_ip_keys+=("${z_bottle}")
   340→    z_ip_vals+=("${z_mon}:bottle")
   341→
   342→    # Subnet non-overlap
   343→    local z_net_int=$(zrbrn_ip_to_int "${z_base}")
   344→    local z_net_mask_bits=$(( (0xFFFFFFFF << (32 - z_mask)) & 0xFFFFFFFF ))
   345→    local z_net_addr=$(( z_net_int & z_net_mask_bits ))
   346→    local z_net_size=$(( 1 << (32 - z_mask) ))
   347→    local z_net_end=$(( z_net_addr + z_net_size - 1 ))
   348→
   349→    local z_k
   350→    for z_k in "${!z_net_starts[@]}"; do
   351→      if [[ ${z_net_addr} -le ${z_net_ends[$z_k]} ]] && [[ ${z_net_starts[$z_k]} -le ${z_net_end} ]]; then
   352→        buc_die "Subnet overlap: ${z_base}/${z_mask} (${z_mon}) overlaps with network of ${z_net_owners[$z_k]}"
   353→      fi
   354→    done
   355→    z_net_starts+=("${z_net_addr}")
   356→    z_net_ends+=("${z_net_end}")
   357→    z_net_owners+=("${z_mon}")
   358→
   359→  done <<< "${z_data}"
   360→}
   361→
   362→# Fleet info table — non-opinionated display of all nameplate configuration
   363→# Requires: RBCC, RBGC, RBGD kindled + RBRR loaded
   364→rbrn_survey() {
   365→  zrbcc_sentinel
   366→  zrbgc_sentinel
   367→  zrbgd_sentinel
   368→  zrbrr_sentinel
   369→
   370→  local z_gar_base="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}/${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
   371→
   372→  echo ""
   373→  printf "%-10s %-8s %6s %6s  %-17s %-14s %-14s  %3s %3s\n" \
   374→    "Moniker" "Entry" "WS" "Enc" "Subnet" "Sentry IP" "Bottle IP" "Snt" "Btl"
   375→  printf "%-10s %-8s %6s %6s  %-17s %-14s %-14s  %3s %3s\n" \
   376→    "--------" "-----" "------" "------" "-----------------" "--------------" "--------------" "---" "---"
   377→
   378→  local z_moniker z_file
   379→  for z_moniker in $(rbrn_list); do
   380→    z_file="${RBCC_KIT_DIR}/${RBCC_RBRN_PREFIX}${z_moniker}${RBCC_RBRN_EXT}"
   381→    (
   382→      source "${z_file}"
   383→
   384→      local z_sentry_img="${z_gar_base}/${RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL}:${RBRN_SENTRY_CONSECRATION}${RBGC_ARK_SUFFIX_IMAGE}"
   385→      local z_bottle_img="${z_gar_base}/${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL}:${RBRN_BOTTLE_CONSECRATION}${RBGC_ARK_SUFFIX_IMAGE}"
   386→
   387→      local z_sentry_local="--"
   388→      local z_bottle_local="--"
   389→      if command -v "${RBRN_RUNTIME}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
   390→        ${RBRN_RUNTIME} image inspect "${z_sentry_img}" >/dev/null 2>&1 && z_sentry_local="ok"
   391→        ${RBRN_RUNTIME} image inspect "${z_bottle_img}" >/dev/null 2>&1 && z_bottle_local="ok"
   392→      else
   393→        z_sentry_local="??"
   394→        z_bottle_local="??"
   395→      fi
   396→
   397→      local z_ws_port="${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION:-}"
   398→      local z_enc_port="${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE:-}"
   399→      if [[ "${RBRN_ENTRY_MODE}" != "enabled" ]]; then
   400→        z_ws_port="-"
   401→        z_enc_port="-"
   402→      fi
   403→
   404→      printf "%-10s %-8s %6s %6s  %-17s %-14s %-14s  %3s %3s\n" \
   405→        "${RBRN_MONIKER}" "${RBRN_ENTRY_MODE}" "${z_ws_port}" "${z_enc_port}" \
   406→        "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP}/${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK}" \
   407→        "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}" "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP}" \
   408→        "${z_sentry_local}" "${z_bottle_local}"
   409→    )
   410→  done
   411→  echo ""
   412→}
   413→
   414→# Audit — survey display then preflight validation
   415→rbrn_audit() {
   416→  rbrn_survey
   417→  rbrn_preflight
   418→  buc_step "Cross-nameplate audit passed"
   419→}
   420→
   421→# eof
   422→

1→#!/bin/bash
     2→#
     3→# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
     4→#
     5→# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");

     8→#
     9→#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
    10→#
    11→# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
    12→# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
    13→# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
    14→# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
    15→# limitations under the License.
    16→#
    17→# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
    18→#
    19→# Recipe Bottle Regime Vessel - Validator Module
    20→
    21→set -euo pipefail
    22→
    23→# Multiple inclusion detection
    24→test -z "${ZRBRV_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbrv multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
    25→ZRBRV_SOURCED=1
    26→
    27→######################################################################
    28→# Internal Functions (zrbrv_*)
    29→
    30→zrbrv_kindle() {
    31→  test -z "${ZRBRV_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbrv already kindled"
    32→
    33→  # Set defaults for all fields (validate enforces required-ness)
    34→  RBRV_SIGIL="${RBRV_SIGIL:-}"
    35→  RBRV_DESCRIPTION="${RBRV_DESCRIPTION:-}"
    36→  RBRV_BIND_IMAGE="${RBRV_BIND_IMAGE:-}"
    37→  RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE="${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE:-}"
    38→  RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT="${RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT:-}"
    39→  RBRV_CONJURE_PLATFORMS="${RBRV_CONJURE_PLATFORMS:-}"
    40→  RBRV_CONJURE_BINFMT_POLICY="${RBRV_CONJURE_BINFMT_POLICY:-}"
    41→
    42→  # Detect unexpected RBRV_ variables
    43→  local z_known="RBRV_SIGIL RBRV_DESCRIPTION RBRV_BIND_IMAGE RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT RBRV_CONJURE_PLATFORMS RBRV_CONJURE_BINFMT_POLICY"
    44→  ZRBRV_UNEXPECTED=()
    45→  local z_var
    46→  for z_var in $(compgen -v RBRV_); do
    47→    case " ${z_known} " in
    48→      *" ${z_var} "*) : ;;
    49→      *) ZRBRV_UNEXPECTED+=("${z_var}") ;;
    50→    esac
    51→  done
    52→
    53→  # Build rollup of all RBRV_ variables for passing to scripts/containers
    54→  ZRBRV_ROLLUP=""
    55→  ZRBRV_ROLLUP+="RBRV_SIGIL='${RBRV_SIGIL}' "
    56→  ZRBRV_ROLLUP+="RBRV_DESCRIPTION='${RBRV_DESCRIPTION}' "
    57→  ZRBRV_ROLLUP+="RBRV_BIND_IMAGE='${RBRV_BIND_IMAGE}' "
    58→  ZRBRV_ROLLUP+="RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE='${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE}' "
    59→  ZRBRV_ROLLUP+="RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT='${RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT}' "
    60→  ZRBRV_ROLLUP+="RBRV_CONJURE_PLATFORMS='${RBRV_CONJURE_PLATFORMS}' "
    61→  ZRBRV_ROLLUP+="RBRV_CONJURE_BINFMT_POLICY='${RBRV_CONJURE_BINFMT_POLICY}'"
    62→
    63→  ZRBRV_KINDLED=1
    64→}
    65→
    66→zrbrv_sentinel() {
    67→  test "${ZRBRV_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbrv not kindled - call zrbrv_kindle first"
    68→}
    69→
    70→# Validate RBRV variables via buv_env_* (dies on first error)
    71→# Prerequisite: kindle must have been called; buv_validation.sh must be sourced
    72→zrbrv_validate_fields() {
    73→  zrbrv_sentinel
    74→
    75→  # Die on unexpected variables
    76→  if test ${#ZRBRV_UNEXPECTED[@]} -gt 0; then
    77→    buc_die "Unexpected RBRV_ variables: ${ZRBRV_UNEXPECTED[*]}"
    78→  fi
    79→
    80→  # Core Vessel Identity
    81→  buv_env_xname       RBRV_SIGIL                   1     64  # Must match directory name
    82→  buv_env_string      RBRV_DESCRIPTION             0    512  # Optional description
    83→
    84→  # Binding Configuration (for copying from registry)
    85→  if test -n "${RBRV_BIND_IMAGE}"; then
    86→    buv_env_fqin      RBRV_BIND_IMAGE              1    512  # Source image to copy
    87→  fi
    88→
    89→  # Conjuring Configuration (for building from source)
    90→  if test -n "${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE}"; then
    91→    buv_env_string    RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE      1    512  # Path relative to repo root
    92→    buv_env_string    RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT      1    512  # Build context relative to repo root
    93→    buv_env_string    RBRV_CONJURE_PLATFORMS       1    512  # Space-separated platforms
    94→    buv_env_string    RBRV_CONJURE_BINFMT_POLICY   1     16  # Either "allow" or "forbid"
    95→    case "${RBRV_CONJURE_BINFMT_POLICY}" in
    96→      allow|forbid) : ;;
    97→      *) buc_die "Invalid RBRV_CONJURE_BINFMT_POLICY: '${RBRV_CONJURE_BINFMT_POLICY}' (must be 'allow' or 'forbid')" ;;
    98→    esac
    99→  fi
   100→
   101→  # Validate at least one operation mode is configured
   102→  if test -z "${RBRV_BIND_IMAGE}" && test -z "${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE}"; then
   103→    buc_die "Vessel must define either RBRV_BIND_IMAGE (for binding) or RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE (for conjuring)"
   104→  fi
   105→}
   106→
   107→# eof
   108→

    16→#
    17→# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
    18→#
    19→# Recipe Bottle Regime Repo - Validator Module
    20→
    21→set -euo pipefail
    22→
    23→# Multiple inclusion detection
    24→test -z "${ZRBRR_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbrr multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
    25→ZRBRR_SOURCED=1
    26→
    27→######################################################################
    28→# Internal Functions (zrbrr_*)
    29→
    30→zrbrr_broach() {
    31→  test -z "${ZRBRR_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbrr already kindled"
    32→
    33→  # Set defaults for all fields (validate enforces required-ness)
    34→  RBRR_VESSEL_DIR="${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR:-}"
    35→  RBRR_DNS_SERVER="${RBRR_DNS_SERVER:-}"
    36→  RBRR_IGNITE_MACHINE_NAME="${RBRR_IGNITE_MACHINE_NAME:-}"
    37→  RBRR_DEPLOY_MACHINE_NAME="${RBRR_DEPLOY_MACHINE_NAME:-}"
    38→  RBRR_CRANE_TAR_GZ="${RBRR_CRANE_TAR_GZ:-}"
    39→  RBRR_MANIFEST_PLATFORMS="${RBRR_MANIFEST_PLATFORMS:-}"
    40→  RBRR_CHOSEN_PODMAN_VERSION="${RBRR_CHOSEN_PODMAN_VERSION:-}"
    41→  RBRR_CHOSEN_VMIMAGE_ORIGIN="${RBRR_CHOSEN_VMIMAGE_ORIGIN:-}"
    42→  RBRR_CHOSEN_IDENTITY="${RBRR_CHOSEN_IDENTITY:-}"
    43→  RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID="${RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID:-}"
    44→  RBRR_GCP_REGION="${RBRR_GCP_REGION:-}"
    45→  RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY="${RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY:-}"
    46→  RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE="${RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE:-}"
    47→  RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT="${RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT:-}"
    48→  RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS="${RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS:-}"
    49→  RBRR_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE="${RBRR_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE:-}"
    50→  RBRR_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE="${RBRR_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE:-}"
    51→  RBRR_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE="${RBRR_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE:-}"
    52→  RBRR_GCB_GCRANE_IMAGE_REF="${RBRR_GCB_GCRANE_IMAGE_REF:-}"
    53→  RBRR_GCB_ORAS_IMAGE_REF="${RBRR_GCB_ORAS_IMAGE_REF:-}"
    54→  RBRR_GCB_GCLOUD_IMAGE_REF="${RBRR_GCB_GCLOUD_IMAGE_REF:-}"
    55→  RBRR_GCB_DOCKER_IMAGE_REF="${RBRR_GCB_DOCKER_IMAGE_REF:-}"
    56→  RBRR_GCB_SKOPEO_IMAGE_REF="${RBRR_GCB_SKOPEO_IMAGE_REF:-}"
    57→  RBRR_GCB_ALPINE_IMAGE_REF="${RBRR_GCB_ALPINE_IMAGE_REF:-}"
    58→  RBRR_GCB_SYFT_IMAGE_REF="${RBRR_GCB_SYFT_IMAGE_REF:-}"
    59→  RBRR_GCB_BINFMT_IMAGE_REF="${RBRR_GCB_BINFMT_IMAGE_REF:-}"
    60→
    61→  # Detect unexpected RBRR_ variables
    62→  local z_known="RBRR_VESSEL_DIR RBRR_DNS_SERVER RBRR_IGNITE_MACHINE_NAME RBRR_DEPLOY_MACHINE_NAME RBRR_CRANE_TAR_GZ RBRR_MANIFEST_PLATFORMS RBRR_CHOSEN_PODMAN_VERSION RBRR_CHOSEN_VMIMAGE_ORIGIN RBRR_CHOSEN_IDENTITY RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID RBRR_GCP_REGION RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS RBRR_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE RBRR_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE RBRR_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE RBRR_GCB_GCRANE_IMAGE_REF RBRR_GCB_ORAS_IMAGE_REF RBRR_GCB_GCLOUD_IMAGE_REF RBRR_GCB_DOCKER_IMAGE_REF RBRR_GCB_SKOPEO_IMAGE_REF RBRR_GCB_ALPINE_IMAGE_REF RBRR_GCB_SYFT_IMAGE_REF RBRR_GCB_BINFMT_IMAGE_REF"
    63→  ZRBRR_UNEXPECTED=()
    64→  local z_var
    65→  for z_var in $(compgen -v RBRR_); do
    66→    case " ${z_known} " in
    67→      *" ${z_var} "*) : ;;
    68→      *) ZRBRR_UNEXPECTED+=("${z_var}") ;;
    69→    esac
    70→  done
    71→
    72→  # Build rollup of all RBRR_ variables for passing to scripts/containers
    73→  ZRBRR_ROLLUP=""
    74→  ZRBRR_ROLLUP+="RBRR_VESSEL_DIR='${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}' "
    75→  ZRBRR_ROLLUP+="RBRR_DNS_SERVER='${RBRR_DNS_SERVER}' "
    76→  ZRBRR_ROLLUP+="RBRR_IGNITE_MACHINE_NAME='${RBRR_IGNITE_MACHINE_NAME}' "
    77→  ZRBRR_ROLLUP+="RBRR_DEPLOY_MACHINE_NAME='${RBRR_DEPLOY_MACHINE_NAME}' "
    78→  ZRBRR_ROLLUP+="RBRR_CRANE_TAR_GZ='${RBRR_CRANE_TAR_GZ}' "
    79→  ZRBRR_ROLLUP+="RBRR_MANIFEST_PLATFORMS='${RBRR_MANIFEST_PLATFORMS}' "
    80→  ZRBRR_ROLLUP+="RBRR_CHOSEN_PODMAN_VERSION='${RBRR_CHOSEN_PODMAN_VERSION}' "
    81→  ZRBRR_ROLLUP+="RBRR_CHOSEN_VMIMAGE_ORIGIN='${RBRR_CHOSEN_VMIMAGE_ORIGIN}' "
    82→  ZRBRR_ROLLUP+="RBRR_CHOSEN_IDENTITY='${RBRR_CHOSEN_IDENTITY}' "
    83→  ZRBRR_ROLLUP+="RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID='${RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}' "
    84→  ZRBRR_ROLLUP+="RBRR_GCP_REGION='${RBRR_GCP_REGION}' "
    85→  ZRBRR_ROLLUP+="RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY='${RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY}' "
    86→  ZRBRR_ROLLUP+="RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE='${RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE}' "
    87→  ZRBRR_ROLLUP+="RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT='${RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT}' "
    88→  ZRBRR_ROLLUP+="RBRR_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE='${RBRR_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE}' "
    89→  ZRBRR_ROLLUP+="RBRR_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE='${RBRR_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}' "
    90→  ZRBRR_ROLLUP+="RBRR_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE='${RBRR_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}' "
    91→  ZRBRR_ROLLUP+="RBRR_GCB_GCRANE_IMAGE_REF='${RBRR_GCB_GCRANE_IMAGE_REF}' "
    92→  ZRBRR_ROLLUP+="RBRR_GCB_ORAS_IMAGE_REF='${RBRR_GCB_ORAS_IMAGE_REF}' "
    93→  ZRBRR_ROLLUP+="RBRR_GCB_GCLOUD_IMAGE_REF='${RBRR_GCB_GCLOUD_IMAGE_REF}' "
    94→  ZRBRR_ROLLUP+="RBRR_GCB_DOCKER_IMAGE_REF='${RBRR_GCB_DOCKER_IMAGE_REF}' "
    95→  ZRBRR_ROLLUP+="RBRR_GCB_SKOPEO_IMAGE_REF='${RBRR_GCB_SKOPEO_IMAGE_REF}' "
    96→  ZRBRR_ROLLUP+="RBRR_GCB_ALPINE_IMAGE_REF='${RBRR_GCB_ALPINE_IMAGE_REF}' "
    97→  ZRBRR_ROLLUP+="RBRR_GCB_SYFT_IMAGE_REF='${RBRR_GCB_SYFT_IMAGE_REF}' "
    98→  ZRBRR_ROLLUP+="RBRR_GCB_BINFMT_IMAGE_REF='${RBRR_GCB_BINFMT_IMAGE_REF}'"
    99→
   100→  # Build docker env args array for container injection
   101→  # Usage: docker run "${ZRBRR_DOCKER_ENV[@]}" ...
   102→  # Currently only RBRR_DNS_SERVER is needed by sentry; add others as needed
   103→  ZRBRR_DOCKER_ENV=()
   104→  ZRBRR_DOCKER_ENV+=("-e" "RBRR_DNS_SERVER=${RBRR_DNS_SERVER}")
   105→
   106→  ZRBRR_KINDLED=1
   107→}
   108→
   109→zrbrr_sentinel() {
   110→  test "${ZRBRR_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbrr not kindled - call zrbrr_broach first"
   111→}
   112→
   113→# Validate RBRR variables via buv_env_* (dies on first error)
   114→# Prerequisite: broach must have been called; buv_validation.sh must be sourced
   115→zrbrr_validate_fields() {
   116→  zrbrr_sentinel
   117→
   118→  # Die on unexpected variables
   119→  if test ${#ZRBRR_UNEXPECTED[@]} -gt 0; then
   120→    buc_die "Unexpected RBRR_ variables: ${ZRBRR_UNEXPECTED[*]}"
   121→  fi
   122→
   123→  # Container Registry Configuration
   124→  buv_env_string      RBRR_VESSEL_DIR              1    255
   125→  buv_env_ipv4        RBRR_DNS_SERVER
   126→
   127→  # Machine Configuration
   128→  buv_env_xname       RBRR_IGNITE_MACHINE_NAME     1     64
   129→  buv_env_xname       RBRR_DEPLOY_MACHINE_NAME     1     64
   130→  buv_env_string      RBRR_CRANE_TAR_GZ            1    512
   131→  buv_env_string      RBRR_MANIFEST_PLATFORMS      1    512
   132→  buv_env_string      RBRR_CHOSEN_PODMAN_VERSION   1     16
   133→  buv_env_fqin        RBRR_CHOSEN_VMIMAGE_ORIGIN   1    256
   134→  buv_env_string      RBRR_CHOSEN_IDENTITY         1    128
   135→  buv_env_gname       RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID          6     63
   136→  buv_env_gname       RBRR_GCP_REGION              1     32
   137→
   138→  # Google Artifact Registry Configuration
   139→  buv_env_gname       RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY          1     63
   140→
   141→  # Google Cloud Build Configuration
   142→  buv_env_string      RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE        3     64
   143→  buv_env_string      RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT             2     10
   144→
   145→  # Service Account Configuration Files
   146→  buv_env_string      RBRR_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE         1    512
   147→  buv_env_string      RBRR_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE     1    512
   148→  buv_env_string      RBRR_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE      1    512
   149→
   150→  # GCB image pins (digest-pinned)
   151→  buv_env_odref       RBRR_GCB_GCRANE_IMAGE_REF
   152→  buv_env_odref       RBRR_GCB_ORAS_IMAGE_REF
   153→  buv_env_odref       RBRR_GCB_GCLOUD_IMAGE_REF
   154→  buv_env_odref       RBRR_GCB_DOCKER_IMAGE_REF
   155→  buv_env_odref       RBRR_GCB_SKOPEO_IMAGE_REF
   156→  buv_env_odref       RBRR_GCB_ALPINE_IMAGE_REF
   157→  buv_env_odref       RBRR_GCB_SYFT_IMAGE_REF
   158→  buv_env_odref       RBRR_GCB_BINFMT_IMAGE_REF
   159→
   160→  # Validate directories exist
   161→  buv_dir_exists "${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}"
   162→
   163→  # Validate manifest platforms format (space-separated identifiers)
   164→  local z_platform=""
   165→  for z_platform in ${RBRR_MANIFEST_PLATFORMS}; do
   166→    if ! printf '%s' "${z_platform}" | grep -q '^[a-z0-9_]\+$'; then
   167→      buc_warn "Invalid platform format in RBRR_MANIFEST_PLATFORMS: ${z_platform}"
   168→      buc_step "Expected format: lowercase alphanumeric with underscores"
   169→      buc_die "Invalid RBRR_MANIFEST_PLATFORMS"
   170→    fi
   171→  done
   172→
   173→  # Validate timeout format (number followed by 's' for seconds)
   174→  if ! printf '%s' "${RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT}" | grep -q '^[0-9]\+s$'; then
   175→    buc_warn "Invalid RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT format: ${RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT}"
   176→    buc_step "Expected format: number followed by 's' (e.g., 1200s)"
   177→    buc_die "Invalid RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT"
   178→  fi
   179→
   180→  # Validate Podman version format (e.g., 5.5 or 5.5.1)
   181→  if ! printf '%s' "${RBRR_CHOSEN_PODMAN_VERSION}" | grep -q '^[0-9]\+\.[0-9]\+\(\.[0-9]\+\)\?$'; then
   182→    buc_warn "Invalid RBRR_CHOSEN_PODMAN_VERSION format: ${RBRR_CHOSEN_PODMAN_VERSION}"
   183→    buc_step "Expected format: semantic version like 5.5 or 5.5.1"
   184→    buc_die "Invalid RBRR_CHOSEN_PODMAN_VERSION"
   185→  fi
   186→}
   187→
   188→######################################################################
   189→# Public Functions (rbrr_*)
   190→
   191→# Load RBRR regime
   192→# Usage: rbrr_load
   193→# Uses RBCC_RBRR_FILE to locate rbrr_RecipeBottleRegimeRepo.sh, verifies exists, sources, broaches, and validates
   194→rbrr_load() {
   195→  local z_rbrr_file="${RBCC_RBRR_FILE}"
   196→  test -f "${z_rbrr_file}" || buc_die "RBRR config not found: ${z_rbrr_file}"
   197→
   198→  source "${z_rbrr_file}" || buc_die "Failed to source RBRR config: ${z_rbrr_file}"
   199→  zrbrr_broach
   200→  zrbrr_validate_fields
   201→}
   202→
   203→# eof
   204→

1→// ⟦axhrb_regime⟧
     2→{rbrr_regime}
     3→
     4→This regime specifies variables that define repository-wide configuration and installation-wide settings within the Recipe Bottle system.
     5→Each
     6→{rbrr_regime}
     7→instance represents foundational infrastructure configuration that rarely changes and is shared across all Recipe Bottle operations.
     8→RBRR assignment files are found in the operator's local filesystem (typically `../station-files/rbrr.env`) and configure depot project identifiers, regional settings, repository locations, and service account credential file paths.
     9→
    10→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
    11→{rbrr_depot_project_id}
    12→
    13→The
    14→{gcp_service}
    15→{gcp_project_id}
    16→where all Recipe Bottle resources are created and managed.
    17→Used as the target
    18→{gcp_project_id}
    19→in API calls and as the audience claim in OAuth token exchange.
    20→
    21→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
    22→{rbrr_gcp_region}
    23→
    24→The default
    25→{gcp_service}
    26→region for regional services like
    27→{gar_service}
    28→and
    29→{gcb_service}.
    30→Applied when creating repositories and submitting builds unless overridden by operation-specific configuration.
    31→
    32→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
    33→{rbrr_gar_repository}
    34→
    35→The
    36→{gar_service}
    37→repository name within
    38→{rbrr_depot_project_id}
    39→that stores all bottle images and metadata.
    40→
    41→// ⟦axhrgb_group⟧
    42→{rbrr_group_service_accounts}
    43→
    44→Service account credential file paths that enable different operational roles.
    45→Each RBRA file contains the minimum identity and signing material needed for authentication to
    46→{gcp_service}
    47→via service account.
    48→
    49→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
    50→{rbrr_retriever_rbra_file}
    51→
    52→Path to an RBRA file stored in the operator's local filesystem outside the source repository.
    53→This file enables the operator to assume the retriever role for container image operations.
    54→
    55→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
    56→{rbrr_director_rbra_file}
    57→
    58→Path to an RBRA file stored in the operator's local filesystem outside the source repository.
    59→This file enables the operator to assume the director role for build submission and image management operations.
    60→
    61→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
    62→{rbrr_governor_rbra_file}
    63→
    64→Path to an RBRA file stored in the operator's local filesystem outside the source repository.
    65→This file enables the operator to assume the governor role for
    66→{gcp_project_id}
    67→administration operations.
    68→
    69→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
    70→{rbrr_payor_rbra_file}
    71→
    72→Path to an RBRA file stored in the operator's local filesystem outside the source repository.
    73→This file enables the operator to assume the payor role for billing and
    74→{gcp_project_id}
    75→lifecycle operations.
    76→
    77→// ⟦axhrgb_group⟧
    78→{rbrr_group_container_runtime}
    79→
    80→Variables that configure container runtime and deployment environment settings.
    81→These settings control local Podman VM configuration, DNS resolution, and build machine selection.
    82→
    83→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
    84→{rbrr_registry_owner}
    85→
    86→GitHub username or organization that owns the container image registry.
    87→Used to construct fully-qualified image references.
    88→
    89→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
    90→{rbrr_registry_name}
    91→
    92→GitHub container registry repository name.
    93→Combined with registry owner to form the base registry path.
    94→
    95→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
    96→{rbrr_vessel_dir}
    97→
    98→Directory path containing vessel configuration subdirectories.
    99→Each subdirectory defines a vessel image source (bind or conjure mode).
   100→
   101→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
   102→{rbrr_dns_server}
   103→
   104→DNS server IP address used by sentry containers for name resolution.
   105→Injected into container environment to control DNS behavior.
   106→
   107→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
   108→{rbrr_ignite_machine_name}
   109→
   110→Podman machine name for bottle ignition (startup) operations.
   111→Identifies which Podman VM to use for local image building and testing.
   112→
   113→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
   114→{rbrr_deploy_machine_name}
   115→
   116→Podman machine name for bottle deployment operations.
   117→Identifies which Podman VM to use for production deployments.
   118→
   119→// ⟦axhrgb_group⟧
   120→{rbrr_group_build_configuration}
   121→
   122→Variables that control container image build processes.
   123→These settings define build tooling, platform targets, timeout constraints, and machine resources for
   124→{gcb_service}
   125→operations.
   126→
   127→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
   128→{rbrr_crane_tar_gz}
   129→
   130→Path to crane tool tarball archive.
   131→Crane is used for OCI image manipulation and registry operations.
   132→
   133→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
   134→{rbrr_manifest_platforms}
   135→
   136→Space-separated list of platforms for multi-platform manifest creation.
   137→Example: `linux/amd64 linux/arm64`.
   138→
   139→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
   140→{rbrr_chosen_podman_version}
   141→
   142→Podman version string for VM provisioning and build operations.
   143→Must match a valid Podman release version.
   144→
   145→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
   146→{rbrr_chosen_vmimage_origin}
   147→
   148→Podman VM image origin specification.
   149→Determines which VM base image to use for machine creation.
   150→
   151→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
   152→{rbrr_chosen_identity}
   153→
   154→Identity specification for VM or build environment.
   155→Used to configure authentication and authorization context.
   156→
   157→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
   158→{rbrr_gcb_machine_type}
   159→
   160→Google Cloud Build machine type specification.
   161→Determines CPU, memory, and performance characteristics for build workers.
   162→Examples: `E2_HIGHCPU_8`, `E2_HIGHCPU_32`.
   163→
   164→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
   165→{rbrr_gcb_min_concurrent_builds}
   166→
   167→Minimum number of concurrent Cloud Build executions required.
   168→The nameplate audit and build preflight query the depot project's *Concurrent Build CPUs* quota via the Service Usage API and verify that `CPU_QUOTA ÷ vCPUs_per_build ≥` this value.
   169→Hard-fails if quota headroom is insufficient.
   170→
   171→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
   172→{rbrr_gcb_timeout}
   173→
   174→Maximum duration for Cloud Build operations before timeout.
   175→Specified in seconds or duration format (e.g., `3600s`).
   176→
   177→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
   178→{rbrr_gcb_gcrane_image_ref}
   179→
   180→Fully-qualified image reference for gcrane tool used in Cloud Build steps.
   181→Gcrane provides Google-authenticated OCI registry operations.
   182→
   183→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
   184→{rbrr_gcb_oras_image_ref}
   185→
   186→Fully-qualified image reference for ORAS tool used in Cloud Build steps.
   187→ORAS handles OCI artifact operations beyond container images.
   188→
   189→// ⟦axhro_broach⟧
   190→{rbkro_broach}
   191→
   192→Sets defaults for all RBRR_ variables via `VAR="${VAR:-}"` pattern.
   193→Detects unexpected RBRR_ variables via compgen prefix scan.
   194→Builds ZRBRR_ROLLUP serialization string and ZRBRR_DOCKER_ENV injection array.
   195→
   196→// ⟦axhro_validate⟧
   197→{rbkro_validate}
   198→
   199→Validates fields via buv_env_* type checkers.
   200→Checks required fields: DEPOT_PROJECT_ID, GCP_REGION, GAR_REPOSITORY.
   201→Validates path existence for RBRA credential files.
   202→Validates machine type and timeout format constraints.
   203→
   204→// ⟦axhro_render⟧
   205→{rbkro_render}
   206→
   207→Renders via rbcr_* shared module.
   208→Sections: GCP Infrastructure, Artifact Registry, Service Account Credentials, Container Runtime Configuration, Build Configuration.
   209→Displays unexpected variables in red.
   210→Validates after full render completes.
   211→

    18→# Recipe Bottle Repository Configuration - Base Values
    19→
    20→RBRR_DNS_SERVER=8.8.8.8
    21→
    22→RBRR_IGNITE_MACHINE_NAME=rbw-vm-ignite
    23→RBRR_DEPLOY_MACHINE_NAME=rbw-vm-deploy
    24→RBRR_CRANE_TAR_GZ=https://github.com/google/go-containerregistry/releases/download/v0.20.3/go-containerregistry_Linux_x86_64.tar.gz
    25→
    26→RBRR_MANIFEST_PLATFORMS="mow_x86_64_wsl mow_aarch64_wsl" # "mos_x86_64_qemu mos_aarch64_applehv"
    27→
    28→RBRR_CHOSEN_PODMAN_VERSION=5.5
    29→RBRR_CHOSEN_VMIMAGE_ORIGIN=quay.io/podman/machine-os-wsl   # Alt is quay.io/podman/machine-os
    30→
    31→RBRR_CHOSEN_IDENTITY=20250723-092042  # mow_x86_64_wsl mow_aarch64_wsl
    32→
    33→# Reference directory containing specs for all vessels: relative to project root
    34→RBRR_VESSEL_DIR=rbev-vessels
    35→
    36→# Google Cloud Platform configuration (shared by all GCP services)
    37→RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID=rbwg-d-proto-251230080456
    38→RBRR_GCP_REGION=us-central1  # Project doesn't have a region but our services do
    39→
    40→# Google Artifact Registry settings
    41→RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY=rbw-proto-repository
    42→
    43→# Google Cloud Build settings  
    44→RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE=UNSPECIFIED            # Google Cloud Build machine type (2 vCPU default; fits 5 concurrent in 10-CPU quota)
    45→RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT=1200s                      # 20 minute timeout
    46→RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS=3            # Minimum concurrent builds required; audit and build preflight verify quota headroom
    47→
    48→########################################################################
    49→# Google Cloud Build tool image pins (digest-pinned, ~spring 2025)
    50→# These are only used inside GCB build steps (_RBGY_* substitutions).
    51→
    52→# gcrane: registry utility (~Apr 2025)
    53→RBRR_GCB_GCRANE_IMAGE_REF="gcr.io/go-containerregistry/gcrane@sha256:b34b7a92b50cb3017af7ae84916c7ca87e04c1766e639fbec6f0656a21d77470"
    54→
    55→# oras: OCI artifact/referrer client (~May 2025)
    56→RBRR_GCB_ORAS_IMAGE_REF="ghcr.io/oras-project/oras@sha256:a3ce6b38d4c510ea9fdc0449b942ea44fb790f157e79b5e7e30b1e7460fe5579"
    57→
    58→# gcloud: GCP CLI builder (~Feb 2026)

    76→
    77→# Service Account Configuration - RBRA (Recipe Bottle Regime Auth) Format
    78→#
    79→# RBRA files are bash-sourceable environment files containing extracted 
    80→# Google service account credentials. No JSON parsing required at runtime.
    81→#
    82→# Required RBRA environment variables:
    83→#   RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL       - Service account email (e.g., sa-name@project.iam.gserviceaccount.com)
    84→#   RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY        - RSA private key in PEM format (includes newlines as \n)
    85→#   RBRA_PROJECT_ID         - GCP project ID for context
    86→#   RBRA_TOKEN_LIFETIME_SEC - OAuth token lifetime in seconds (300-3600)
    87→#
    88→# Security notes:
    89→#   - RBRA files contain raw credentials - protect like passwords
    90→#   - Never commit RBRA files to version control
    91→#   - Use shortest reasonable TOKEN_LIFETIME_SEC for each role
    92→#
    93→# Service accounts by role:
    94→#
    95→# 1. Pull/list container images
    96→RBRR_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE=../station-files/secrets/rbra-retriever.env
    97→#    Permissions: roles/artifactregistry.reader
    98→#    Token lifetime: 300 seconds (quick pulls)
    99→#
   100→# 2. Submit Cloud Build image jobs, delete images
   101→RBRR_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE=../station-files/secrets/rbra-director.env
   102→#    Permissions: roles/cloudbuild.builds.editor + storage.admin on staging
   103→#    Token lifetime: 600 seconds (build submission)
   104→#
   105→# 3. GOVERNOR
   106→RBRR_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE=../station-files/secrets/rbra-governor.env
   107→#    Permissions: roles/owner (FULL PROJECT CONTROL)
   108→#    Token lifetime: 1800 seconds (complex setup operations)
   109→
   110→
   111→# eof
   112→
