    42→
    43→== Introduction
    44→
    45→A
    46→{crg_regime}
    47→is a set of files that work together to define allowed
    48→{crg_variable_s}
    49→and check that actual
    50→{crg_value_s}
    51→follow the rules.
    52→Each
    53→{crg_variable}
    54→defines a name and rules constraining valid
    55→{crg_value_s}.
    56→{crg_variable_s}
    57→may be members of
    58→{crg_group_s}.
    59→Some groups can be turned on or off using a
    60→{crg_enable}.
    61→
    62→These files define the rules and structure:
    63→
    64→. The
    65→{crg_spec_adoc}
    66→defines each
    67→{crg_variable}
    68→and how it relates to others in a structured asciidoc format.
    69→This document serves as the master reference for what
    70→{crg_value_s}
    71→are valid.
    72→. The
    73→{crg_service_mk}
    74→contains make rules to check that
    75→{crg_value_s}
    76→follow their
    77→{crg_variable}
    78→definitions.
    79→. Bash scripts check and display
    80→{crg_value_s}:
    81→.. The
    82→{crg_validator_sh}
    83→checks that
    84→{crg_value_s}
    85→follow their rules
    86→.. The
    87→{crg_renderer_sh}
    88→shows the
    89→{crg_value_s}
    90→in a clear format
    91→.. The
    92→{crg_library_sh}
    93→provides helper functions used by both scripts, typically providing per
    94→{crg_variable}
    95→type level utilities.
    96→. The
    97→{crg_glossary_adoc}
    98→gives plain English descriptions of each {crg_variable}.
    99→
   100→These files contain actual {crg_value_s} that follow the rules:
   101→
   102→. An
   103→{crg_assignment_mk}
   104→declares real
   105→{crg_value_s}
   106→for the
   107→{crg_variable_s}
   108→defined by the
   109→{crg_regime}.
   110→A project may have several of these files, each holding a different set of
   111→{crg_value_s}
   112→that all follow the same rules.
   113→. An
   114→{crg_assignment_sh}
   115→is just like a
   116→{crg_assignment_mk},
   117→but works in bash scripts as well as make.
   118→
   119→== {crg_spec_adoc} Requirements
   120→
   121→=== Scope and Structure
   122→
   123→* An opening term reference section that:
   124→** Uses ONLY those parent specification's attribute references needed to express configuration requirements clearly
   125→** Uses parent references exactly as they appear in source, without modification
   126→** Documents source specification in section header comment
   127→** DOES NOT include Assignment Variable mappings
   128→
   129→* {crg_group}
   130→sections, each containing:
   131→** Purpose statement
   132→** {crg_variable}
   133→definitions using this exact table structure:
   134→+
   135→[source,asciidoc]
   136→----
   137→.VARIABLE_NAME
   138→[cols="1,4"]
   139→|===
   140→|Purpose|
   141→<clear description of variable purpose>
   142→|Required|
   143→Yes/No
   144→|Format|
   145→<format specification and constraints, if applicable>
   146→|Dependencies|
   147→<other required variables or conditions, if applicable>
   148→|Example|
   149→export VARIABLE_NAME := <example value>
   150→|===
   151→----
   152→+
   153→*** Required fields (Purpose, Required) must always be present
   154→*** Format, Dependencies, and Example fields included only when applicable
   155→*** Tables must maintain proper AsciiDoc syntax and spacing
   156→*** Variable name must appear as table title
   157→
   158→* Term definitions section that:
   159→** Contains asciidoc dentries ONLY for parent terms referenced in the document
   160→** Uses exact anchor names from parent specification
   161→** Copies parent term definitions verbatim, without modification
   162→** Includes ONLY those terms needed to express configuration requirements clearly
   163→
   164→The {crg_spec_adoc} DOES NOT include:
   165→
   166→* Implementation details
   167→* Error handling
   168→* Security requirements  
   169→* Operational procedures
   170→* System architecture
   171→
   172→=== Organization Requirements
   173→
   174→* Groups
   175→{crg_variable}
   176→logically by feature
   177→* Indicates dependencies clearly
   178→* States enable flag requirements explicitly
   179→* Maintains consistent structure across groups
   180→* Uses Asciidoc format
   181→* Applies consistent formatting throughout
   182→
   183→Each 
   184→{crg_group}:
   185→
   186→* Provides clear group purpose
   187→* Presents variables in logical order
   188→* Shows relationships between member variables
   189→* Includes complete example configurations
   190→
   191→=== Variable Declaration
   192→
   193→An
   194→{crg_assignment_mk}:
   195→
   196→* Places each
   197→{crg_variable}
   198→on its own line
   199→* Uses `=` operator
   200→* Does not use `export` directive
   201→
   202→=== Assignment Variable Names
   203→
   204→Each
   205→{crg_variable}
   206→name:
   207→
   208→* Begins with
   209→{crg_prefix}
   210→* Contains only uppercase letters, numbers, and underscores after prefix
   211→* Excludes spaces and special characters
   212→
   213→=== Assignment Variable Values
   214→
   215→{crg_variable}
   216→values:
   217→
   218→* Contain no leading or trailing whitespace
   219→* Use forward slashes for path separations
   220→* Contain no unexpanded environment variable references
   221→* Use immediate expansion via `:=`
   222→
   223→=== Organization
   224→
   225→An
   226→{crg_assignment_mk}:
   227→
   228→* Groups variables by related purpose
   229→* Uses comments to describe each group's purpose
   230→* Separates logical groups with blank lines
   231→
   232→
   233→== {crg_service_mk} Requirements
   234→
   235→=== Basic Structure
   236→
   237→A
   238→{crg_service_mk}:
   239→
   240→* Groups all rules and functions by
   241→{crg_group}
   242→* Uses lower case
   243→{crg_prefix}
   244→for external interface targets
   245→* Does NOT use `.PHONY`
   246→
   247→=== Core Required Targets
   248→
   249→==== Validation Target (<regime>_validate)
   250→
   251→TODO
   252→
   253→==== Render Target (<regime>_render)
   254→
   255→TODO
   256→
   257→==== Rollup Environment Variable Declaration
   258→
   259→The
   260→{crg_service_mk}
   261→provides a standardized declaration for a consolidated environment variable that starts with
   262→{crg_prefix}:
   263→
   264→[source,makefile]
   265→----
   266→# Container environment arguments for Assignment Variables
   267→<make_assign_prefix>_ROLLUP_ENVIRONMENT_VAR := \
   268→  VARIABLE_1='$(VARIABLE_1)' \
   269→  VARIABLE_2='$(VARIABLE_2)' \
   270→  VARIABLE_3='$(VARIABLE_3)'
   271→
   272→----
   273→
   274→The declaration:
   275→
   276→. Uses the
   277→{crg_prefix}
   278→with `_ROLLUP_ENVIRONMENT_VAR` suffix, i.e. a double underscore
   279→. Uses := operator for immediate expansion
   280→. Lists every
   281→{crg_variable}
   282→defined in the
   283→{crg_spec_adoc}
   284→without conditional statements
   285→. Uses line continuation and consistent indentation
   286→. Includes all variables regardless of their optional status
   287→
   288→== {crg_glossary_adoc} Requirements
   289→
   290→The
   291→{crg_glossary_adoc}
   292→follows this exact pattern:
   293→
   294→[source,asciidoc,opts=novalidate]
   295→----
   296→// tag::mapping-section[]
   297→
   298→// Assignment Variables are mapped using the pattern:
   299→:rbn_variable_name:              <<rbn_variable_name,RBN_VARIABLE_NAME>>
   300→
   301→// end::mapping-section[]
   302→
   303→== <Regime Name> Definitions
   304→
   305→[[rbn_variable_name]]
   306→{rbn_variable_name}::
   307→<simple-english-assignment-variable-definition>
   308→----
   309→
   310→The
   311→{crg_glossary_adoc}:
   312→
   313→* Defines each
   314→{crg_variable}
   315→in simple English
   316→* Uses snake_case term anchors derived from the variable names
   317→* References variables using curly brace notation (e.g., {rbn_volume_mounts})
   318→* Creates explicit mappings for consistent cross-referencing
   319→
   320→== Implementation Guidelines
   321→
   322→A
   323→{crg_service_mk}:
   324→
   325→* Declares
   326→{crg_prefix} and {crg_prefix}
   327→as comments, not as makefile variables, near the top:
   328→[source,makefile]
   329→----
   330→# Regime Prefix: xyz_
   331→# Assignment Prefix: XYZ_
   332→----
   333→
   334→* Excludes recursive make calls ($(MAKE))
   335→* Places each recipe step on a single line
   336→* Groups code by
   337→{crg_group}
   338→
   339→== Core Terms
   340→
   341→The following terms have specific meanings within configuration management:
   342→
   343→[[crg_regime]]
   344→{crg_regime}::
   345→A structured system for defining, validating, and rendering sets of
   346→{crg_variable_s}
   347→through a coordinated set of specification and implementation components.
   348→Contains multiple
   349→{crg_group_s}
   350→which organize related configuration values.
   351→
   352→[[crg_variable]]
   353→{crg_variable}::
   354→A named parameter defined in a
   355→{crg_regime}
   356→that specifies requirements for its
   357→{crg_value_s}.
   358→Each
   359→{crg_variable}
   360→defines its name, format constraints, and relationships with other variables.
   361→All
   362→{crg_variable}
   363→names begin with their uppercase
   364→{crg_prefix}
   365→and consist only of uppercase letters, numbers, and underscores.
   366→
   367→[[crg_value]]
   368→{crg_value}::
   369→A specific assignment in a
   370→{crg_assignment_mk}
   371→or
   372→{crg_assignment_sh}
   373→that sets a
   374→{crg_variable}
   375→to a particular value.
   376→Each
   377→{crg_value}
   378→must conform to its
   379→{crg_variable_p}
   380→requirements as defined in the
   381→{crg_spec_adoc}
   382→and enforced by the
   383→{crg_validator_sh}.
   384→
   385→[[crg_prefix]]
   386→{crg_prefix}::
   387→
   388→A distinct sequence of alphanumeric characters with exactly one trailing underscore that connects all
   389→{crg_variable_s}
   390→belonging to a specific
   391→{crg_regime}.
   392→{crg_variable_s}
   393→use the uppercase form (e.g., `XYZ_`) while
   394→{crg_library_sh} utilities and rules use the lowercase form (e.g., `xyz_`).
   395→This prefix prevents namespace collisions between different
   396→{crg_regime_s}.
   397→
   398→[[crg_group]]
   399→{crg_group}::
   400→A set of related
   401→{crg_variable_s}
   402→that together define a complete configuration aspect, often activated or deactivated by an
   403→{crg_enable}.
   404→
   405→[[crg_enable]]
   406→{crg_enable}::
   407→A boolean
   408→{crg_variable}
   409→that controls whether a
   410→{crg_group}
   411→is active. When enabled, all group variables must satisfy their validation rules.
   412→When disabled, group variables are not validated.
   413→
   414→[[crg_assignment_mk]]
   415→{crg_assignment_mk}::
   416→A bilingual makefile/shell script that declares specific values for a set of
   417→{crg_variable_s},
   418→forming a complete configuration instance that must conform to the requirements defined in the
   419→{crg_spec_adoc}.
   420→Each variable declaration must avoid spaces around `=` to maintain compatibility with both make and shell interpretation.
   421→This file cannot include others.
   422→Additionally, any syntax expansion using a previous
   423→{crg_variable}
   424→must use `${...}` as in:
   425→----
   426→MBV_TOOLS_DIR=Tools
   427→MBV_CONSOLE_MAKEFILE=${MBV_TOOLS_DIR}/rbw.workbench.mk
   428→
   429→----
   430→
   431→[[crg_assignment_sh]]
   432→{crg_assignment_sh}::
   433→A limited bash script that declares specific values for a set of
   434→{crg_variable_s}.
   435→forming a complete configuration instance that must conform to the requirements defined in the
   436→{crg_spec_adoc}.
   437→
   438→[[crg_spec_adoc]]
   439→{crg_spec_adoc}::
   440→An asciidoc document that defines all valid
   441→{crg_variable_s}
   442→and their relationships within a
   443→{crg_regime}.
   444→This specification serves as the authoritative reference for configuration requirements.
   445→
   446→[[crg_glossary_adoc]]
   447→{crg_glossary_adoc}::
   448→An asciidoc document that specifies a
   449→{crg_prefix} and provides clear, simple English definitions for each
   450→{crg_variable}
   451→while establishing consistent term mappings that can be referenced throughout the documentation.
   452→
   453→[[crg_service_mk]]
   454→{crg_service_mk}::
   455→A makefile that provides standardized targets for validating and rendering {crg_variable_s} imported from an
   456→{crg_assignment_mk}.
   457→It orchestrates the workflow by invoking the
   458→{crg_validator_sh} and {crg_renderer_sh}
   459→scripts, which in turn use the
   460→{crg_library_sh}.
   461→
   462→[[crg_rollup]]
   463→{crg_rollup}::
   464→A consolidated make variable in the
   465→{crg_service_mk}
   466→that collects all
   467→{crg_variable_s}
   468→in a format suitable for passing to bash scripts, enabling consistent access to configuration values across the implementation components.
   469→
   470→[[crg_validator_sh]]
   471→{crg_validator_sh}::
   472→A bash script that performs comprehensive validation of
   473→{crg_variable_s}
   474→according to rules defined in the
   475→{crg_spec_adoc}.
   476→It uses functions from the
   477→{crg_library_sh}
   478→for basic type checking while implementing regime-specific relationship validation.
   479→
   480→[[crg_renderer_sh]]
   481→{crg_renderer_sh}::
   482→A bash script that formats and displays
   483→{crg_variable_s}
   484→in a clear, structured way. It assumes input has passed validation through
   485→{crg_validator_sh}
   486→and focuses solely on presentation concerns.
   487→
   488→[[crg_library_sh]]
   489→{crg_library_sh}::
   490→A shared bash library providing common functions for validation and rendering operations across multiple
   491→{crg_regime_s}.
   492→It offers stable, tested implementations of frequently needed operations.
   493→
   494→== Core Type Definitions
   495→
   496→// Configuration Regime Type definitions
   497→=== Atomic Types
   498→
   499→[[crg_atom_string]]
   500→{crg_atom_string}::
   501→A sequence of characters potentially including spaces and special characters, though no newlines.
   502→
   503→[[crg_atom_xname]]
   504→{crg_atom_xname}::
   505→A system-safe identifier that must start with a letter and contain only letters, numbers, underscores, and hyphens.
   506→
   507→[[crg_atom_fqin]]
   508→{crg_atom_fqin}::
   509→A Fully Qualified Image Name component that must start with a letter or number and may contain letters, numbers, colons, dots, underscores, hyphens, and forward slashes.
   510→
   511→[[crg_atom_bool]]
   512→{crg_atom_bool}::
   513→A binary value that must be either 0 or 1.
   514→
   515→[[crg_atom_decimal]]
   516→{crg_atom_decimal}::
   517→A non-empty decimal number that must fall within a specified minimum and maximum range.
   518→
   519→[[crg_atom_ipv4]]
   520→{crg_atom_ipv4}::
   521→A valid IPv4 address in dotted decimal notation (e.g., 192.168.1.1).
   522→
   523→[[crg_atom_cidr]]
   524→{crg_atom_cidr}::
   525→A valid IPv4 CIDR block specification combining an IPv4 address with a network prefix length (e.g., 192.168.1.0/24).
   526→
   527→[[crg_atom_domain]]
   528→{crg_atom_domain}::
   529→A valid domain name that must start and end with alphanumeric characters and may contain dots and hyphens between them.
   530→
   531→[[crg_atom_port]]
   532→{crg_atom_port}::
   533→A network port number that must be between 1 and 65535.
   534→
   535→[glossary]
   536→=== List Types
   537→
   538→[[crg_list_ipv4]]
   539→{crg_list_ipv4}::
   540→A space-separated list of zero or more IPv4 addresses.
   541→
   542→[[crg_list_cidr]]
   543→{crg_list_cidr}::
   544→A space-separated list of zero or more CIDR block specifications.
   545→
   546→[[crg_list_domain]]
   547→{crg_list_domain}::
   548→A space-separated list of zero or more domain names.
   549→
   550→

    17→| 4 | RBRR_ | Regime Repo | RBW | Repository | Git-tracked, sourced at workbench kindle | `rbw/rbrr_regime.sh` | `rbrr_RecipeBottleRegimeRepo.sh` | `RBRR-RegimeRepo.adoc` |
    18→| 5 | RBRN_ | Regime Nameplate | RBW | Per-service | Git-tracked, one file per nameplate | `rbw/rbrn_regime.sh` | `rbw/rbrn_nsproto.env`, `rbrn_srjcl.env`, `rbrn_pluml.env` | `RBRN-RegimeNameplate.adoc` |
    19→| 6 | RBRP_ | Regime Payor | RBW | GCP payor project | Git-tracked | `rbw/rbrp_regime.sh` | `rbrp.env` | -- |
    20→| 7 | RBRE_ | Regime ECR | RBW | AWS ECR credentials | External (NOT in git) | `rbw/rbre_regime.sh` | External | -- |

    23→| 10 | RBRV_ | Regime Vessel | RBW | Per-vessel build/bind | Git-tracked, one per vessel dir | `rbw/rbrv_regime.sh` | `rbev-vessels/*/rbrv.env` (6 vessels) | -- |
    24→| 11 | RBRA_ | Credential Format | RBW | Per-service-account | External (NOT in git) | -- (no validator) | Written by `rbgp_Payor.sh` / `rbgg_Governor.sh` | -- |

This is a more recent refactoring that collapsed 5 boolean flags into 2 mode enums plus the existing ENTRY_MODE.

   231→
   232→## Cross-Regime Dependency Map
   233→
   234→```
   235→BURC ─── sources ──► BURS  (via BURC_STATION_FILE path)
   236→  │
   237→  └─── feeds ──► BURD  (dispatch reads BURC, creates BURD_ runtime vars)
   238→
   239→RBRR ─── references ──► RBRA  (3 file paths: GOVERNOR, RETRIEVER, DIRECTOR)
   240→  │
   241→  └─── depends on ──► RBGC  (Constants, for naming patterns)
   242→
   243→RBRP ─── depends on ──► RBGC  (zrbgc_sentinel required before kindle)
   244→
   245→RBRN ─── independent  (no cross-regime validator dependencies)
   246→RBRE ─── independent
   247→RBRG ─── independent
   248→RBRS ─── independent
   249→RBRV ─── independent
   250→
   251→RBRA ─── written by ──► RBGP (Payor), RBGG (Governor)
   252→     ─── consumed by ──► RBGO (OAuth)
   253→     ─── paths in ──► RBRR (3 path variables)
   254→```
   255→
   256→### Kindle Order (enforced by sentinel calls)
   257→
   258→1. **RBGC** (Constants) -- must be first, depended on by RBRP
   259→2. **BURC** -- infrastructure, enables BURS
   260→3. **BURS** -- station, enables BUD logging
   261→4. **RBRR** -- repo config, enables RBRA file access
   262→5. **RBRP** -- payor config (needs RBGC)
   263→6. All others are independent and can kindle in any order
   264→
   265→---
   266→
   267→## Naming Convention Analysis (per CRR Framework)
   268→
   269→### CRR Compliance Assessment
   270→
   271→| Regime | Has Prefix | Has Validator | Has Assignment | Has Spec Doc | Has Glossary | CRR Compliant |
   272→|--------|-----------|---------------|----------------|-------------|-------------|---------------|
   273→| BURC_ | Yes | Yes | Yes | No | No | Partial |
   274→| BURS_ | Yes | Yes | Yes (external) | No | No | Partial |
   275→| BURD_ | Yes | No (runtime) | No | No | No | N/A (not a regime) |
   276→| RBRR_ | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | No | Mostly |
   277→| RBRN_ | Yes | Yes | Yes (3 files) | Yes | No | Mostly |
   278→| RBRP_ | Yes | Yes | Yes | No | No | Partial |
   279→| RBRE_ | Yes | Yes | No (external) | No | No | Partial |
   280→| RBRG_ | Yes | Yes | No (external) | No | No | Partial |
   281→| RBRS_ | Yes | Yes | No (external) | No | No | Partial |
   282→| RBRV_ | Yes | Yes | Yes (6 files) | No | No | Partial |
   283→| RBRA_ | Yes | No | N/A | No | No | N/A (not a regime) |
   284→
   285→### Prefix Naming Patterns
   286→
   287→All regimes follow the mint discipline:
   288→- **BUK domain:** `BU` + one-char suffix: `BURC`, `BURS`, `BUD`
   289→- **RBW domain:** `RBR` + one-char suffix: `RBRR`, `RBRN`, `RBRP`, `RBRE`, `RBRG`, `RBRS`, `RBRV`, `RBRA`
   290→
   291→Terminal exclusivity is maintained -- no prefix is both a leaf and a parent.
   292→
   293→### Validator Pattern Consistency
   294→
   295→All validators follow a uniform structure:
   296→1. Copyright header
   297→2. `set -euo pipefail`
   298→3. Multiple inclusion guard (`Z{PREFIX}_SOURCED`)
   299→4. `z{prefix}_kindle()` function with `Z{PREFIX}_KINDLED` guard
   300→5. Uses `buv_env_*` validation functions (from BUK validation utilities)
   301→6. `z{prefix}_sentinel()` function for downstream callers
   302→7. Rollup construction (RBRN, RBRV only)
   303→
   304→---
   305→
   306→## Gap Analysis
   307→
   308→### Missing Spec Documents (5 regimes)
   309→
   310→| Regime | Impact | Recommendation |
   311→|--------|--------|----------------|
   312→| BURC_ | Medium | Create `BURC-RegimeConfiguration.adoc` -- well-defined scope, 10 variables |
   313→| RBRP_ | Low | Create `RBRP-RegimePayor.adoc` -- small (3 vars), but payor setup is critical path |
   314→| RBRE_ | Low | External credentials; spec may not add value |
   315→| RBRG_ | Low | External credentials; spec may not add value |
   316→| RBRS_ | Low | Create `RBRS-RegimeStation.adoc` -- small but documents workstation requirements |
   317→
   318→### Missing Glossary Documents (all regimes)
   319→
   320→No regime has a CRR-compliant `{crg_glossary_adoc}`. RBRN and RBRR have spec docs that serve some of this purpose, but formal glossary files with `tag::mapping-section[]` and linked term definitions don't exist.
   321→
   322→### Missing Service Makefiles (all regimes)
   323→
   324→CRR defines `{crg_service_mk}` with validation and render targets. No regime has one -- all validation is done via bash validators called at kindle time. This is a design divergence: the project uses bash-first validation rather than make-based validation.
   325→
   326→### Validators Without Full Type Coverage
   327→
   328→- RBRP: Uses custom regex validation instead of `buv_env_*` for all 3 variables
   329→- RBRR: Uses `buv_env_gname` and `buv_env_odref` which aren't in CRR's type system (project extensions)
   330→- BUD: No validation at all (runtime-only)
   331→
   332→### RBRV Mode Semantics
   333→
   334→RBRV uses a mutual-presence pattern (bind vs conjure) rather than an explicit mode enum. Both can be set simultaneously (the validator allows it). This differs from RBRN's explicit `_MODE` enum pattern. Consider whether RBRV should have an explicit `RBRV_MODE` variable (`bind`/`conjure`/`both`).
   335→
   336→### RBRN Spec-Validator Drift
   337→
   338→The RBRN spec doc still documents boolean flags (`ENTRY_ENABLED`, `DNS_ENABLED`, `ACCESS_ENABLED`, `DNS_GLOBAL`, `ACCESS_GLOBAL`) but the validator now uses tri-state `_MODE` enums. The spec needs updating to match the current validator.
   339→
   340→### Credential Regime Gap
   341→
   342→RBRA is used consistently (4 variables, written by 2 producers, consumed by 1 consumer) but has no validator. Since credential files are high-value targets, a validator would catch format errors early. Consider creating `rbra_regime.sh`.
   343→

1→# Assessment: axvr_*/axhr*_ Regime Annotation Patterns for RBRN
     2→
     3→**Pace:** ₢ARAAQ (assess-rbrn-regime-fit)
     4→**Heat:** ₣AR (rbw-ark-vessel-vocabulary)
     5→**Date:** 2026-02-06
     6→**Inputs:** RBRN, AXLA (with axvr_*/axhr*_ terms from ₢ARAAN), RBSRV (exemplar from ₢ARAAP)
     7→
     8→---
     9→
    10→## RBRN Structure Summary
    11→
    12→RBRN has six feature groups with three distinct conditionality patterns:
    13→
    14→| Section | Variables | Conditionality |
    15→|---------|-----------|----------------|
    16→| Core Service Identity | MONIKER, DESCRIPTION, RUNTIME | Unconditional |
    17→| Ark Reference | SENTRY_VESSEL, SENTRY_CONSECRATION, BOTTLE_VESSEL, BOTTLE_CONSECRATION | Unconditional |
    18→| Entry Service | ENTRY_ENABLED, ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION, ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE | Simple gate (ENTRY_ENABLED=1) |
    19→| Enclave Network | ENCLAVE_BASE_IP, ENCLAVE_NETMASK, ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP, ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP | Unconditional |
    20→| Uplink | PORT_MIN, DNS_ENABLED, ACCESS_ENABLED, DNS_GLOBAL, ACCESS_GLOBAL, ALLOWED_CIDRS, ALLOWED_DOMAINS | Mixed: 5 unconditional + 2 compound-conditional |
    21→| Volume Mount | VOLUME_MOUNTS | Unconditional |
    22→
    23→---
    24→
    25→## Q1: Do unconditional groups work with axhrgb_group without axhrgc_gate?
    26→
    27→**YES — fully supported.**
    28→
    29→AXLA's Regime Hierarchy Consistency rules (lines 2618-2624) explicitly address this:
    30→
    31→> `axvr_group` without `axd_conditional`: corresponding `axhrgb_group` must have no `axhrgc_gate`
    32→
    33→So in the parent document (RBAGS), define:
    34→```asciidoc
    35→[[rbrn_group_core]]
    36→//axvr_group
    37→{rbrn_group_core}::
    38→Core identity variables for {rbrn_regime}.
    39→```
    40→
    41→Note: no `axd_conditional` dimension. Then in the RBRN subdocument:
    42→```asciidoc
    43→//axhrgb_group
    44→{rbrn_group_core}
    45→
    46→//axhrgv_variable
    47→{rbrn_moniker}
    48→...
    49→```
    50→
    51→No `axhrgc_gate` — the group is unconditional.
    52→
    53→**This contrasts with RBSRV**, where both groups (Binding, Conjuring) are conditional and gated on `vessel_mode`. RBRN proves the unconditional path works.
    54→
    55→---
    56→
    57→## Q2: Does stacked axhrgc_gate handle RBRN's compound conditions?
    58→
    59→**YES — mechanically sound, with a caveat on boolean typing (see Q3).**
    60→
    61→RBRN's compound conditions:
    62→
    63→- `ALLOWED_CIDRS`: Required when `ACCESS_ENABLED=1 AND ACCESS_GLOBAL=0`
    64→- `ALLOWED_DOMAINS`: Required when `DNS_ENABLED=1 AND DNS_GLOBAL=0`
    65→
    66→AXLA nesting rules (line 2017): "Multiple axhrgc_gate within one group have AND semantics"
    67→
    68→Proposed structure for CIDRS (assuming boolean→enum conversion per Q3):
    69→
    70→```asciidoc
    71→//axhrgb_group
    72→{rbrn_group_access_allowlist}
    73→
    74→//axhrgc_gate
    75→{rbrn_uplink_access_enabled} {rbrn_uplink_access_enabled_on}
    76→
    77→//axhrgc_gate
    78→{rbrn_uplink_access_global} {rbrn_uplink_access_global_off}
    79→
    80→//axhrgv_variable
    81→{rbrn_uplink_allowed_cidrs}
    82→```
    83→
    84→The stacked gates read: "this group is active when access_enabled=on AND access_global=off."
    85→
    86→**Key insight:** The two compound conditions (CIDRS vs DOMAINS) use DIFFERENT gate variables, so they need SEPARATE groups — you cannot combine them into one group.
    87→
    88→---
    89→
    90→## Q3: Boolean variables as gates — axt_boolean vs axt_enumeration?
    91→
    92→**axhrgc_gate REQUIRES axt_enumeration. Booleans must be promoted.**
    93→
    94→The axhrgc_gate definition (AXLA line 1983-1984) explicitly states:
    95→> "the enumerated variable (voicing axt_enumeration) and the specific axt_enum_value that activates this gate"
    96→
    97→RBRN has **5 boolean flags** that serve as gate variables:
    98→
    99→| Current Variable | Current Type | Role |
   100→|------------------|-------------|------|
   101→| RBRN_ENTRY_ENABLED | crg_atom_bool | Gates Entry group |
   102→| RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_ENABLED | crg_atom_bool | Gates DNS allowlist |
   103→| RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_ENABLED | crg_atom_bool | Gates access allowlist |
   104→| RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_GLOBAL | crg_atom_bool | Gates DNS allowlist (compound) |
   105→| RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_GLOBAL | crg_atom_bool | Gates access allowlist (compound) |
   106→
   107→Each must become an enumeration with explicit values. Term proliferation:
   108→
   109→- **Per boolean → 3 terms**: variable + on-value + off-value
   110→- **5 booleans × 3 = 15 terms** (vs 5 today)
   111→
   112→### Recommendation: Standard binary enumeration pattern
   113→
   114→Rather than ad-hoc naming, establish a convention:
   115→- `{rbrn_entry_enabled}` voices `axt_enumeration`
   116→- `{rbrn_entry_enabled_on}` voices `axt_enum_value` (maps to `1`)
   117→- `{rbrn_entry_enabled_off}` voices `axt_enum_value` (maps to `0`)
   118→
   119→The `_on`/`_off` suffix convention is consistent and self-documenting. The underlying bash `.env` values remain `1` and `0` — only the AXLA vocabulary changes.
   120→
   121→### Alternative considered and rejected
   122→
   123→Extending `axhrgc_gate` to accept `axt_boolean` would reduce proliferation but violates the gate's semantic contract (gates select among enumerated alternatives, not binary states). The boolean-as-enum approach is more principled: even two-valued choices are choices.
   124→
   125→---
   126→
   127→## Q4: RBRN patterns that expose gaps in axhr*_ design
   128→
   129→### Gap 1: Mixed conditionality within a logical section
   130→
   131→RBRN's "Uplink" section contains:
   132→- 5 unconditional variables (PORT_MIN, DNS_ENABLED, ACCESS_ENABLED, DNS_GLOBAL, ACCESS_GLOBAL)
   133→- 2 variables with different compound conditions (ALLOWED_CIDRS, ALLOWED_DOMAINS)
   134→
   135→This doesn't map to a single group. The required structure:
   136→
   137→```
   138→Ungrouped:   rbrn_uplink_port_min
   139→Ungrouped:   rbrn_uplink_dns_enabled
   140→Ungrouped:   rbrn_uplink_access_enabled
   141→Ungrouped:   rbrn_uplink_dns_global
   142→Ungrouped:   rbrn_uplink_access_global
   143→Group A:     rbrn_group_access_allowlist  (gated: access_enabled=on AND access_global=off)
   144→  Variable:  rbrn_uplink_allowed_cidrs
   145→Group B:     rbrn_group_dns_allowlist     (gated: dns_enabled=on AND dns_global=off)
   146→  Variable:  rbrn_uplink_allowed_domains
   147→```
   148→
   149→**This is valid but breaks the RBRN document's current visual grouping.** The original RBRN presents "Uplink Configuration" as one section. Under axhr, the 5 unconditional vars become ungrouped (`axhrv_variable`), and the 2 conditional vars get their own separate groups.
   150→
   151→**Verdict:** Not a gap per se — the system works. But it reveals that axhr hierarchies decompose around conditionality, not around human-readable sections. Document authors need to accept that the hierarchy structure may not mirror the original document's visual layout.
   152→
   153→### Gap 2: Single-variable conditional groups
   154→
   155→Both `rbrn_group_access_allowlist` and `rbrn_group_dns_allowlist` contain exactly ONE variable each. Creating a full group+gate structure for a single variable feels heavyweight.
   156→
   157→**Verdict:** Livable. The group exists because the conditionality exists. A group with one variable is degenerate but not incorrect. This is a consequence of Q5's trade-off.
   158→
   159→### Gap 3: Boolean-to-enumeration proliferation (see Q3)
   160→
   161→5 boolean gate variables × 3 terms each = significant vocabulary growth. This is a real cost.
   162→
   163→**Verdict:** Accepted cost. The alternative (extending the gate mechanism) is worse.
   164→
   165→---
   166→
   167→## Q5: Would variable-level axd_conditional help?
   168→
   169→**Variable-level conditionality would reduce verbosity for RBRN's Uplink pattern but is NOT recommended at this time.**
   170→
   171→### What it would look like
   172→
   173→A hypothetical `axhrv_variable` with inline gate:
   174→```asciidoc
   175→//axhrv_variable axd_conditional(rbrn_uplink_access_enabled rbrn_uplink_access_enabled_on, rbrn_uplink_access_global rbrn_uplink_access_global_off)
   176→{rbrn_uplink_allowed_cidrs}
   177→```
   178→
   179→This eliminates the single-variable group wrapper.
   180→
   181→### Why not now
   182→
   183→1. **RBSRV didn't need it** — groups worked naturally there
   184→2. **RBRN is the first regime to expose the pattern** — premature to generalize from one case
   185→3. **Adds parser complexity** — gates are currently positional (next N attribute references); variable-level gates need different syntax
   186→4. **Groups have value even with one variable** — they name the concept ("access allowlist") which aids readability
   187→
   188→### Recommendation
   189→
   190→Apply the group-level approach for RBRN now. If a third regime reveals the same single-variable-conditional pattern, revisit variable-level conditionality as a refinement.
   191→
   192→---
   193→
   194→## Summary: What works, what needs adjustment, recommendations
   195→
   196→### Works as-is
   197→- Unconditional groups (axhrgb_group without axhrgc_gate) ✓
   198→- Stacked AND gates for compound conditions ✓
   199→- Ungrouped variables (axhrv_variable) for truly independent vars ✓
   200→- Cross-document validation rules apply cleanly ✓
   201→
   202→### Needs adjustment before RBRN application
   203→- **Boolean gate variables → enumerations**: 5 variables must be retyped. Establish `_on`/`_off` naming convention.
   204→- **RBAGS parent document**: Needs `rbrn_*` voicing annotations (axvr_regime, axvr_variable, axvr_group) matching the new structure.
   205→- **Type voicings**: RBRN's CRR types (crg_atom_bool, etc.) need mapping to RBAGS type voicings (rbst_*) or AXLA types.
   206→
   207→### Recommended RBRN group structure
   208→
   209→| axhr Structure | Variables | Conditionality |
   210→|----------------|-----------|----------------|
   211→| Ungrouped (axhrv_variable) | moniker, description, runtime | — |
   212→| Group: rbrn_group_ark_reference | sentry_vessel, sentry_consecration, bottle_vessel, bottle_consecration | Unconditional |
   213→| Group: rbrn_group_entry | entry_port_workstation, entry_port_enclave | Conditional: entry_enabled=on |
   214→| Ungrouped (axhrv_variable) | entry_enabled | — (it's the gate, not gated) |
   215→| Ungrouped (axhrv_variable) | enclave_base_ip, enclave_netmask, enclave_sentry_ip, enclave_bottle_ip | — |
   216→| Ungrouped (axhrv_variable) | uplink_port_min, dns_enabled, access_enabled, dns_global, access_global | — |
   217→| Group: rbrn_group_access_allowlist | uplink_allowed_cidrs | Conditional: access_enabled=on AND access_global=off |
   218→| Group: rbrn_group_dns_allowlist | uplink_allowed_domains | Conditional: dns_enabled=on AND dns_global=off |
   219→| Ungrouped (axhrv_variable) | volume_mounts | — |
   220→
   221→**Note:** Gate variables (entry_enabled, dns_enabled, access_enabled, dns_global, access_global) are ungrouped because they're always required — they ARE the enablers, not the enabled.
   222→
   223→### Design consideration: ungrouped enclave variables
   224→
   225→An alternative is to make "enclave" an unconditional group. Whether to group or leave ungrouped depends on whether "enclave network configuration" is a meaningful sub-concept of the nameplate regime worth naming, or just a collection of related-but-independent variables. Both approaches are valid under axhr. Recommend grouping if RBAGS will define the concept, ungrouped if they're just implementation details.
   226→
   227→---
   228→
   229→## Next steps (for future paces)
   230→
   231→1. Mint `rbrn_*` terms in RBAGS (voicing annotations for regime, variables, groups, enum values)
   232→2. Mint `rbst_*` type voicings if not already covering RBRN's types
   233→3. Create/retrofit RBRN subdocument (or RBRN itself) with axhr hierarchy markers
   234→4. Boolean → enumeration conversion for 5 gate variables
   235→

1→#!/bin/bash
     2→#
     3→# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
     4→#
     5→# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
     6→# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
     7→# You may obtain a copy of the License at
     8→#
     9→#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
    10→#
    11→# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
    12→# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
    13→# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
    14→# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
    15→# limitations under the License.
    16→#
    17→# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
    18→#
    19→# RBRN CLI - Command line interface for RBRN nameplate operations
    20→
    21→set -euo pipefail
    22→
    23→ZRBRN_CLI_SCRIPT_DIR="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"
    24→
    25→# Source dependencies
    26→source "${ZRBRN_CLI_SCRIPT_DIR}/../buk/buc_command.sh"
    27→source "${ZRBRN_CLI_SCRIPT_DIR}/../buk/buv_validation.sh"
    28→source "${ZRBRN_CLI_SCRIPT_DIR}/rbrn_regime.sh"
    29→source "${ZRBRN_CLI_SCRIPT_DIR}/rbcc_Constants.sh"
    30→source "${ZRBRN_CLI_SCRIPT_DIR}/rbcr_render.sh"
    31→
    32→######################################################################
    33→# CLI Functions
    34→
    35→zrbrn_cli_kindle() {
    36→  test -z "${ZRBRN_CLI_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "RBRN CLI already kindled"
    37→  ZRBRN_CLI_KINDLED=1
    38→}
    39→
    40→# Command: validate - source file and validate (dies on first error)
    41→rbrn_validate() {
    42→  local z_file="${1:-}"
    43→  test -n "${z_file}" || buc_die "rbrn_validate: file argument required"
    44→  test -f "${z_file}" || buc_die "rbrn_validate: file not found: ${z_file}"
    45→
    46→  buc_step "Validating RBRN nameplate file: ${z_file}"
    47→
    48→  # Use rbrn_load_file for standardized loading
    49→  rbrn_load_file "${z_file}"
    50→
    51→  buc_step "RBRN nameplate valid"
    52→}
    53→
    54→# Command: render - diagnostic display then validate
    55→rbrn_render() {
    56→  local z_file="${1:-}"
    57→  test -n "${z_file}" || buc_die "rbrn_render: file argument required"
    58→  test -f "${z_file}" || buc_die "rbrn_render: file not found: ${z_file}"
    59→
    60→  # Source and kindle (no dying — show all fields before validation)
    61→  source "${z_file}" || buc_die "rbrn_render: failed to source ${z_file}"
    62→  zrbrn_kindle
    63→  zrbcr_kindle
    64→
    65→  # Display header
    66→  echo ""
    67→  echo "${ZBUC_WHITE}RBRN - Recipe Bottle Regime Nameplate${ZBUC_RESET}"
    68→  echo "${ZBUC_WHITE}File: ${z_file}${ZBUC_RESET}"
    69→  echo ""
    70→
    71→  # Core Service Identity
    72→  rbcr_section_begin "Core Service Identity"
    73→  rbcr_section_item RBRN_MONIKER              xname   req  "Unique identifier for Bottle Service"
    74→  rbcr_section_item RBRN_DESCRIPTION          string  opt  "Human-readable description"
    75→  rbcr_section_item RBRN_RUNTIME              enum    req  "Container runtime: docker or podman"
    76→  rbcr_section_end
    77→
    78→  # Container Image Configuration
    79→  rbcr_section_begin "Container Image Configuration"
    80→  rbcr_section_item RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL        fqin    req  "Vessel identifier for Sentry Image"
    81→  rbcr_section_item RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL        fqin    req  "Vessel identifier for Bottle Image"
    82→  rbcr_section_item RBRN_SENTRY_CONSECRATION  fqin    req  "Consecration tag for Sentry Image"
    83→  rbcr_section_item RBRN_BOTTLE_CONSECRATION  fqin    req  "Consecration tag for Bottle Image"
    84→  rbcr_section_end
    85→
    86→  # Entry Service Configuration (gated by ENTRY_MODE)
    87→  rbcr_section_begin "Entry Service Configuration" RBRN_ENTRY_MODE enabled
    88→  rbcr_section_item RBRN_ENTRY_MODE              enum  req   "Entry functionality: disabled or enabled"
    89→  rbcr_section_item RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION  port  cond  "External port on Transit Network"
    90→  rbcr_section_item RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE      port  cond  "Enclave port between Sentry and Bottle"
    91→  rbcr_section_end
    92→
    93→  # Enclave Network Configuration
    94→  rbcr_section_begin "Enclave Network Configuration"
    95→  rbcr_section_item RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP      ipv4     req  "Base IPv4 for enclave network"
    96→  rbcr_section_item RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK      decimal  req  "Network mask width (8-30)"
    97→  rbcr_section_item RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP    ipv4     req  "IP address for Sentry Container"
    98→  rbcr_section_item RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP    ipv4     req  "IP address for Bottle Container"
    99→  rbcr_section_end
   100→
   101→  # Uplink Core
   102→  rbcr_section_begin "Uplink Core"
   103→  rbcr_section_item RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN      port  req  "Minimum port for outbound connections"
   104→  rbcr_section_item RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE      enum  req  "DNS mode: disabled, global, or allowlist"
   105→  rbcr_section_item RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE   enum  req  "IP access mode: disabled, global, or allowlist"
   106→  rbcr_section_end
   107→
   108→  # Uplink DNS Allowlist (gated by DNS_MODE)
   109→  rbcr_section_begin "Uplink DNS Allowlist" RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE allowlist
   110→  rbcr_section_item RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS  domain_list  cond  "Allowed DNS domains"
   111→  rbcr_section_end
   112→
   113→  # Uplink Access Allowlist (gated by ACCESS_MODE)
   114→  rbcr_section_begin "Uplink Access Allowlist" RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE allowlist
   115→  rbcr_section_item RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS  cidr_list  cond  "Allowed CIDR ranges"
   116→  rbcr_section_end
   117→
   118→  # Volume Mount Configuration
   119→  rbcr_section_begin "Volume Mount Configuration"
   120→  rbcr_section_item RBRN_VOLUME_MOUNTS  string  opt  "Volume mount arguments for Bottle"
   121→  rbcr_section_end
   122→
   123→  # Unexpected variables (from kindle, not gated)
   124→  if test ${#ZRBRN_UNEXPECTED[@]} -gt 0; then
   125→    echo "${ZBUC_RED}Unexpected RBRN_ variables:${ZBUC_RESET}"
   126→    local z_var
   127→    for z_var in "${ZRBRN_UNEXPECTED[@]}"; do
   128→      printf "  ${ZBUC_RED}%-30s${ZBUC_RESET} = %s\n" "${z_var}" "${!z_var:-}"
   129→    done
   130→    echo ""
   131→  fi
   132→
   133→  # Validate (dies on first error, after full display)
   134→  zrbrn_validate_fields
   135→  echo "${ZBUC_GREEN}RBRN nameplate valid${ZBUC_RESET}"
   136→}
   137→
   138→######################################################################
   139→# Main dispatch
   140→
   141→zrbrn_cli_kindle
   142→zrbcc_kindle
   143→
   144→z_command="${1:-}"
   145→z_moniker="${2:-}"
   146→
   147→case "${z_command}" in
   148→  validate|render)
   149→    if test -z "${z_moniker}"; then
   150→      buc_step "Available nameplates:"
   151→      rbrn_list | while read -r z_m; do
   152→        echo "  ${z_m}"
   153→      done
   154→    else
   155→      z_file="${RBCC_KIT_DIR}/${RBCC_RBRN_PREFIX}${z_moniker}${RBCC_RBRN_EXT}"
   156→      test -f "${z_file}" || buc_die "Nameplate not found: ${z_file}"
   157→      case "${z_command}" in
   158→        validate) rbrn_validate "${z_file}" ;;
   159→        render)   rbrn_render "${z_file}" ;;
   160→      esac
   161→    fi
   162→    ;;
   163→  survey|audit)
   164→    # Additional dependencies for cross-nameplate operations
   165→    source "${ZRBRN_CLI_SCRIPT_DIR}/rbgc_Constants.sh"
   166→    source "${ZRBRN_CLI_SCRIPT_DIR}/rbgd_DepotConstants.sh"
   167→    source "${ZRBRN_CLI_SCRIPT_DIR}/rbrr_regime.sh"
   168→    source "${ZRBRN_CLI_SCRIPT_DIR}/rbgo_OAuth.sh"
   169→    zrbgc_kindle
   170→    rbrr_load
   171→    zrbgo_kindle
   172→    zrbgd_kindle
   173→    case "${z_command}" in
   174→      survey) rbrn_survey ;;
   175→      audit)
   176→        rbrn_audit
   177→        # GCB quota headroom check (requires Director SA token)
   178→        z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBRR_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
   179→          || buc_die "Failed to get token for GCB quota check"
   180→        rbgd_check_gcb_quota "${z_token}"
   181→        buc_step "Full audit passed (nameplates + GCB quota)"
   182→        ;;
   183→    esac
   184→    ;;
   185→  *)
   186→    buc_die "Unknown command: ${z_command}. Usage: rbrn_cli.sh {validate|render|survey|audit} [moniker]"
   187→    ;;
   188→esac
   189→
   190→# eof
   191→

     2→#

    14→# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
    15→# limitations under the License.
    16→#
    17→# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
    18→#
    19→# RBRV CLI - Command line interface for RBRV vessel operations
    20→
    21→set -euo pipefail
    22→
    23→ZRBRV_CLI_SCRIPT_DIR="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"
    24→
    25→# Source dependencies
    26→source "${ZRBRV_CLI_SCRIPT_DIR}/../buk/buc_command.sh"
    27→source "${ZRBRV_CLI_SCRIPT_DIR}/../buk/buv_validation.sh"
    28→source "${ZRBRV_CLI_SCRIPT_DIR}/rbrv_regime.sh"
    29→source "${ZRBRV_CLI_SCRIPT_DIR}/rbcc_Constants.sh"
    30→source "${ZRBRV_CLI_SCRIPT_DIR}/rbrr_regime.sh"
    31→source "${ZRBRV_CLI_SCRIPT_DIR}/rbcr_render.sh"
    32→
    33→######################################################################
    34→# CLI Functions
    35→
    36→zrbrv_cli_kindle() {
    37→  test -z "${ZRBRV_CLI_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "RBRV CLI already kindled"
    38→  ZRBRV_CLI_KINDLED=1
    39→}
    40→
    41→# Command: validate - source file and validate (dies on first error)
    42→rbrv_validate() {
    43→  local z_file="${1:-}"
    44→  test -n "${z_file}" || buc_die "rbrv_validate: file argument required"
    45→  test -f "${z_file}" || buc_die "rbrv_validate: file not found: ${z_file}"
    46→
    47→  buc_step "Validating RBRV vessel file: ${z_file}"
    48→
    49→  # Source the assignment file
    50→  source "${z_file}" || buc_die "rbrv_validate: failed to source ${z_file}"
    51→
    52→  # Prepare state (no dying)
    53→  zrbrv_kindle
    54→
    55→  # Strict validation (dies on error)
    56→  zrbrv_validate_fields
    57→
    58→  buc_step "RBRV vessel valid"
    59→}
    60→
    61→# Command: render - diagnostic display then validate
    62→rbrv_render() {
    63→  local z_file="${1:-}"
    64→  test -n "${z_file}" || buc_die "rbrv_render: file argument required"
    65→  test -f "${z_file}" || buc_die "rbrv_render: file not found: ${z_file}"
    66→
    67→  # Source and kindle (no dying — show all fields before validation)
    68→  source "${z_file}" || buc_die "rbrv_render: failed to source ${z_file}"
    69→  zrbrv_kindle
    70→  zrbcr_kindle
    71→
    72→  # Display header
    73→  echo ""
    74→  echo "${ZBUC_WHITE}RBRV - Recipe Bottle Regime Vessel${ZBUC_RESET}"
    75→  echo "${ZBUC_WHITE}File: ${z_file}${ZBUC_RESET}"
    76→  echo ""
    77→
    78→  # Core Vessel Identity
    79→  rbcr_section_begin "Core Vessel Identity"
    80→  rbcr_section_item RBRV_SIGIL        xname   req  "Unique identifier (must match directory name)"
    81→  rbcr_section_item RBRV_DESCRIPTION  string  opt  "Human-readable description"
    82→  rbcr_section_end
    83→
    84→  # Binding Configuration
    85→  rbcr_section_begin "Binding Configuration"
    86→  rbcr_section_item RBRV_BIND_IMAGE  fqin  opt  "Source image to copy from registry"
    87→  rbcr_section_end
    88→
    89→  # Conjuring Configuration
    90→  rbcr_section_begin "Conjuring Configuration"
    91→  rbcr_section_item RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE     string  opt  "Dockerfile path relative to repo root"
    92→  rbcr_section_item RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT     string  opt  "Build context relative to repo root"
    93→  rbcr_section_item RBRV_CONJURE_PLATFORMS      string  opt  "Space-separated target platforms"
    94→  rbcr_section_item RBRV_CONJURE_BINFMT_POLICY  enum    opt  "Cross-platform policy: allow or forbid"
    95→  rbcr_section_end
    96→
    97→  # Unexpected variables (from kindle, not gated)
    98→  if test ${#ZRBRV_UNEXPECTED[@]} -gt 0; then
    99→    echo "${ZBUC_RED}Unexpected RBRV_ variables:${ZBUC_RESET}"
   100→    local z_var
   101→    for z_var in "${ZRBRV_UNEXPECTED[@]}"; do
   102→      printf "  ${ZBUC_RED}%-30s${ZBUC_RESET} = %s\n" "${z_var}" "${!z_var:-}"
   103→    done
   104→    echo ""
   105→  fi
   106→
   107→  # Validate (dies on first error, after full display)
   108→  zrbrv_validate_fields
   109→  echo "${ZBUC_GREEN}RBRV vessel valid${ZBUC_RESET}"
   110→}
   111→
   112→######################################################################
   113→# Main dispatch
   114→
   115→zrbrv_cli_kindle
   116→zrbcc_kindle
   117→
   118→z_command="${1:-}"
   119→z_sigil="${2:-}"
   120→
   121→case "${z_command}" in
   122→  validate|render)
   123→    if test -z "${z_sigil}"; then
   124→      # Load RBRR to get RBRR_VESSEL_DIR
   125→      test -f "${RBCC_RBRR_FILE}" || buc_die "RBRR regime file not found: ${RBCC_RBRR_FILE}"
   126→      rbrr_load
   127→      buc_step "Available vessels:"
   128→      for z_d in "${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}"/*/; do
   129→        test -d "${z_d}" || continue
   130→        test -f "${z_d}/rbrv.env" || continue
   131→        z_s="${z_d%/}"
   132→        z_s="${z_s##*/}"
   133→        echo "  ${z_s}"
   134→      done
   135→    else
   136→      # Load RBRR to get RBRR_VESSEL_DIR for path resolution
   137→      test -f "${RBCC_RBRR_FILE}" || buc_die "RBRR regime file not found: ${RBCC_RBRR_FILE}"
   138→      rbrr_load
   139→      z_file="${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${z_sigil}/rbrv.env"
   140→      test -f "${z_file}" || buc_die "Vessel not found: ${z_file}"
   141→      case "${z_command}" in
   142→        validate) rbrv_validate "${z_file}" ;;
   143→        render)   rbrv_render "${z_file}" ;;
   144→      esac
   145→    fi
   146→    ;;
   147→  *)
   148→    buc_die "Unknown command: ${z_command}. Usage: rbrv_cli.sh {validate|render} [sigil]"
   149→    ;;
   150→esac
   151→
   152→# eof
   153→

    19→# RBRR CLI - Command line interface for RBRR repo regime operations
    20→
    21→set -euo pipefail
    22→
    23→ZRBRR_CLI_SCRIPT_DIR="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"
    24→
    25→# Source dependencies
    26→source "${ZRBRR_CLI_SCRIPT_DIR}/../buk/buc_command.sh"
    27→source "${ZRBRR_CLI_SCRIPT_DIR}/../buk/buv_validation.sh"
    28→source "${ZRBRR_CLI_SCRIPT_DIR}/rbrr_regime.sh"
    29→source "${ZRBRR_CLI_SCRIPT_DIR}/rbcc_Constants.sh"
    30→source "${ZRBRR_CLI_SCRIPT_DIR}/rbcr_render.sh"
    31→
    32→######################################################################
    33→# CLI Functions
    34→
    35→zrbrr_cli_kindle() {
    36→  test -z "${ZRBRR_CLI_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "RBRR CLI already kindled"
    37→
    38→  # GCB runs on linux/amd64 — pin digests must match this platform
    39→  ZRBRR_CLI_PIN_OS="linux"
    40→  ZRBRR_CLI_PIN_ARCH="amd64"
    41→
    42→  ZRBRR_CLI_KINDLED=1
    43→}
    44→
    45→# Command: validate - source file and validate (dies on first error)
    46→rbrr_validate() {
    47→  local z_file="${1:-}"
    48→  test -n "${z_file}" || buc_die "rbrr_validate: file argument required"
    49→  test -f "${z_file}" || buc_die "rbrr_validate: file not found: ${z_file}"
    50→
    51→  buc_step "Validating RBRR repo regime file: ${z_file}"
    52→
    53→  # Use rbrr_load for standardized loading
    54→  rbrr_load
    55→
    56→  buc_step "RBRR repo regime valid"
    57→}
    58→
    59→# Command: render - diagnostic display then validate
    60→rbrr_render() {
    61→  local z_file="${1:-}"
    62→  test -n "${z_file}" || buc_die "rbrr_render: file argument required"
    63→  test -f "${z_file}" || buc_die "rbrr_render: file not found: ${z_file}"
    64→
    65→  # Source and kindle (no dying — show all fields before validation)
    66→  source "${z_file}" || buc_die "rbrr_render: failed to source ${z_file}"
    67→  zrbrr_broach
    68→  zrbcr_kindle
    69→
    70→  # Display header
    71→  echo ""
    72→  echo "${ZBUC_WHITE}RBRR - Recipe Bottle Regime Repo${ZBUC_RESET}"
    73→  echo "${ZBUC_WHITE}File: ${z_file}${ZBUC_RESET}"
    74→  echo ""
    75→
    76→  # Vessel and Local Configuration
    77→  rbcr_section_begin "Vessel and Local Configuration"
    78→  rbcr_section_item RBRR_VESSEL_DIR              string  req  "Vessel definitions directory"
    79→  rbcr_section_item RBRR_DNS_SERVER              ipv4    req  "DNS server for containers"
    80→  rbcr_section_item RBRR_IGNITE_MACHINE_NAME     xname   req  "Podman machine for ignite operations"
    81→  rbcr_section_item RBRR_DEPLOY_MACHINE_NAME     xname   req  "Podman machine for deploy operations"
    82→  rbcr_section_end
    83→
    84→  # Build Tool Configuration
    85→  rbcr_section_begin "Build Tool Configuration"
    86→  rbcr_section_item RBRR_CRANE_TAR_GZ            string  req  "Crane binary archive path"
    87→  rbcr_section_item RBRR_MANIFEST_PLATFORMS      string  req  "Target platforms for manifests"
    88→  rbcr_section_item RBRR_CHOSEN_PODMAN_VERSION   string  req  "Podman version (semantic version)"
    89→  rbcr_section_item RBRR_CHOSEN_VMIMAGE_ORIGIN   fqin    req  "VM image origin reference"
    90→  rbcr_section_item RBRR_CHOSEN_IDENTITY         string  req  "Identity for operations"
    91→  rbcr_section_end
    92→
    93→  # GCP Infrastructure
    94→  rbcr_section_begin "GCP Infrastructure"
    95→  rbcr_section_item RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID        gname   req  "GCP project ID for depot"
    96→  rbcr_section_item RBRR_GCP_REGION              gname   req  "GCP region"
    97→  rbcr_section_item RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY          gname   req  "Google Artifact Registry repository name"
    98→  rbcr_section_end
    99→
   100→  # Google Cloud Build Configuration
   101→  rbcr_section_begin "Google Cloud Build Configuration"
   102→  rbcr_section_item RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE        string  req  "Machine type for Cloud Build"
   103→  rbcr_section_item RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT             string  req  "Build timeout (e.g., 1200s)"
   104→  rbcr_section_item RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS string req "Min concurrent builds required"
   105→  rbcr_section_item RBRR_GCB_GCRANE_IMAGE_REF    odref   req  "gcrane image reference (digest-pinned)"
   106→  rbcr_section_item RBRR_GCB_ORAS_IMAGE_REF      odref   req  "oras image reference (digest-pinned)"
   107→  rbcr_section_item RBRR_GCB_GCLOUD_IMAGE_REF    odref   req  "gcloud image reference (digest-pinned)"
   108→  rbcr_section_item RBRR_GCB_DOCKER_IMAGE_REF    odref   req  "docker image reference (digest-pinned)"
   109→  rbcr_section_item RBRR_GCB_SKOPEO_IMAGE_REF    odref   req  "skopeo image reference (digest-pinned)"
   110→  rbcr_section_item RBRR_GCB_ALPINE_IMAGE_REF    odref   req  "alpine image reference (digest-pinned)"
   111→  rbcr_section_item RBRR_GCB_SYFT_IMAGE_REF      odref   req  "syft image reference (digest-pinned)"
   112→  rbcr_section_item RBRR_GCB_BINFMT_IMAGE_REF    odref   req  "binfmt image reference (digest-pinned)"
   113→  rbcr_section_end
   114→
   115→  # Service Account Configuration
   116→  rbcr_section_begin "Service Account Configuration"
   117→  rbcr_section_item RBRR_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE      string  req  "Governor service account key file"
   118→  rbcr_section_item RBRR_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE     string  req  "Retriever service account key file"
   119→  rbcr_section_item RBRR_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE      string  req  "Director service account key file"
   120→  rbcr_section_end
   121→
   122→  # Unexpected variables (from kindle, not gated)
   123→  if test ${#ZRBRR_UNEXPECTED[@]} -gt 0; then
   124→    echo "${ZBUC_RED}Unexpected RBRR_ variables:${ZBUC_RESET}"
   125→    local z_var
   126→    for z_var in "${ZRBRR_UNEXPECTED[@]}"; do
   127→      printf "  ${ZBUC_RED}%-30s${ZBUC_RESET} = %s\n" "${z_var}" "${!z_var:-}"
   128→    done
   129→    echo ""
   130→  fi
   131→
   132→  # Validate (dies on first error, after full display)
   133→  zrbrr_validate_fields
   134→  echo "${ZBUC_GREEN}RBRR repo regime valid${ZBUC_RESET}"
   135→}
   136→
   137→# Command: refresh_gcb_pins - resolve image tags to digests and update RBRR file
   138→# Every step must succeed or the function dies — no partial updates.
   139→# Requires: docker, jq, curl, RBCC_RBRR_FILE set, BURD_NOW_STAMP set, BURD_TEMP_DIR set
   140→rbrr_refresh_gcb_pins() {
   141→  local z_rbrr_file="${RBCC_RBRR_FILE}"
   142→  test -f "${z_rbrr_file}" || buc_die "RBRR config not found: ${z_rbrr_file}"
   143→  test -n "${BURD_NOW_STAMP:-}" || buc_die "BURD_NOW_STAMP not set - must be called from BURD"
   144→  test -d "${BURD_TEMP_DIR:-}" || buc_die "BURD_TEMP_DIR not set or not a directory"
   145→
   146→  buc_countdown 5 "NOTE: Docker anonymous login is heavily rate limited.  Try again in 6 hours if you see that fail.  Continuing in:"
   147→
   148→  local z_vintage="${BURD_NOW_STAMP}"
   149→  local z_oras_token_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbrr_oras_token.json"
   150→  local z_oras_tags_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbrr_oras_tags.json"
   151→  local z_oras_token_value_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbrr_oras_token_value.txt"
   152→  local z_oras_tag_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbrr_oras_tag.txt"
   153→  local z_refresh_prefix="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbrr_refresh_"
   154→  local z_refresh_sed_prefix="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbrr_refresh_sed_"
   155→
   156→  # Discover latest oras stable version from GHCR API.
   157→  # oras doesn't publish a :latest tag, so we must find the newest semver release.
   158→  # GHCR requires a bearer token even for public images.
   159→  buc_step "Discovering latest oras version from GHCR (no :latest tag published)"
   160→
   161→  buc_log_args "Obtaining GHCR bearer token (anonymous, scoped to oras repo)"
   162→  curl -sS \
   163→    "https://ghcr.io/token?scope=repository:oras-project/oras:pull&service=ghcr.io" \
   164→    -o "${z_oras_token_file}" 2>/dev/null \
   165→    || buc_die "Failed to fetch GHCR bearer token"
   166→
   167→  jq -r '.token // empty' "${z_oras_token_file}" > "${z_oras_token_value_file}" \
   168→    || buc_die "Failed to extract token from GHCR response"
   169→  local z_oras_token
   170→  z_oras_token=$(<"${z_oras_token_value_file}")
   171→  test -n "${z_oras_token}" || buc_die "GHCR returned empty bearer token"
   172→
   173→  buc_log_args "Fetching tag list using bearer token"
   174→  curl -sS \
   175→    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${z_oras_token}" \
   176→    "https://ghcr.io/v2/oras-project/oras/tags/list?n=1000" \
   177→    -o "${z_oras_tags_file}" 2>/dev/null \
   178→    || buc_die "Failed to fetch oras tags from GHCR"
   179→
   180→  buc_log_args "Extracting newest stable semver tag"
   181→  # Filters to exact vN.N.N (no pre-release suffixes), sorts numerically, takes last
   182→  jq -r '
   183→    [.tags[] | select(test("^v[0-9]+\\.[0-9]+\\.[0-9]+$"))]
   184→    | map(ltrimstr("v") | split(".") | map(tonumber))
   185→    | sort_by(.[0], .[1], .[2])
   186→    | last
   187→    | "v\(.[0]).\(.[1]).\(.[2])"
   188→  ' "${z_oras_tags_file}" > "${z_oras_tag_file}" \
   189→    || buc_die "Failed to extract semver tag from oras tags response"
   190→  local z_oras_tag
   191→  z_oras_tag=$(<"${z_oras_tag_file}")
   192→  test -n "${z_oras_tag}" -a "${z_oras_tag}" != "null" \
   193→    || buc_die "No stable oras semver tag found in GHCR tag list"
   194→  buc_info "oras discovered tag: ${z_oras_tag}"
   195→
   196→  # Image specifications: VARNAME|BASE_IMAGE|TAG
   197→  # Most images use :latest which always points to newest version.
   198→  # oras uses discovered semver tag above (no :latest published).
   199→  local z_specs=(
   200→    "RBRR_GCB_GCRANE_IMAGE_REF|gcr.io/go-containerregistry/gcrane|latest"
   201→    "RBRR_GCB_ORAS_IMAGE_REF|ghcr.io/oras-project/oras|${z_oras_tag}"
   202→    "RBRR_GCB_GCLOUD_IMAGE_REF|gcr.io/cloud-builders/gcloud|latest"
   203→    "RBRR_GCB_DOCKER_IMAGE_REF|gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker|latest"
   204→    "RBRR_GCB_SKOPEO_IMAGE_REF|quay.io/skopeo/stable|latest"
   205→    "RBRR_GCB_ALPINE_IMAGE_REF|docker.io/library/alpine|latest"
   206→    "RBRR_GCB_SYFT_IMAGE_REF|docker.io/anchore/syft|latest"
   207→    "RBRR_GCB_BINFMT_IMAGE_REF|docker.io/tonistiigi/binfmt|latest"
   208→  )
   209→
   210→  buc_step "Refreshing GCB tool image pins (vintage: ~${z_vintage})"
   211→
   212→  local z_updated=0
   213→  local z_unchanged=0
   214→
   215→  local z_spec=""
   216→  local z_varname=""
   217→  local z_image=""
   218→  local z_tag=""
   219→  local z_manifest_file=""
   220→  local z_digest_file=""
   221→  local z_oldref_file=""
   222→  local z_digest=""
   223→  local z_full_ref=""
   224→  local z_old_ref=""
   225→  local z_sed_value_file=""
   226→  local z_sed_vintage_file=""
   227→  for z_spec in "${z_specs[@]}"; do
   228→    IFS='|' read -r z_varname z_image z_tag <<< "${z_spec}"
   229→
   230→    z_manifest_file="${z_refresh_prefix}${z_varname}_manifest.json"
   231→    z_digest_file="${z_refresh_prefix}${z_varname}_digest.txt"
   232→    z_oldref_file="${z_refresh_prefix}${z_varname}_oldref.txt"
   233→    z_sed_value_file="${z_refresh_sed_prefix}${z_varname}_value.sh"
   234→    z_sed_vintage_file="${z_refresh_sed_prefix}${z_varname}_vintage.sh"
   235→
   236→    buc_step "Inspecting ${z_image}:${z_tag}"
   237→
   238→    docker manifest inspect --verbose "${z_image}:${z_tag}" > "${z_manifest_file}" 2>/dev/null \
   239→      || buc_die "Failed to fetch manifest for ${z_image}:${z_tag}"
   240→
   241→    buc_log_args "Extracting ${ZRBRR_CLI_PIN_OS}/${ZRBRR_CLI_PIN_ARCH} digest from manifest"
   242→    jq -r --arg os "${ZRBRR_CLI_PIN_OS}" --arg arch "${ZRBRR_CLI_PIN_ARCH}" '
   243→      if type == "array" then
   244→        [.[] | select(.Descriptor.platform.architecture == $arch
   245→                  and .Descriptor.platform.os == $os)][0].Descriptor.digest
   246→      else
   247→        .Descriptor.digest
   248→      end' \
   249→      "${z_manifest_file}" > "${z_digest_file}" \
   250→      || buc_die "Failed to extract digest for ${z_image}:${z_tag}"
   251→    z_digest=$(<"${z_digest_file}")
   252→    test -n "${z_digest}" || buc_die "Empty digest for ${z_image}:${z_tag}"
   253→    z_full_ref="${z_image}@${z_digest}"
   254→
   255→    grep "^${z_varname}=" "${z_rbrr_file}" | cut -d'"' -f2 > "${z_oldref_file}" \
   256→      || buc_die "No existing value for ${z_varname} in rbrr file"
   257→    z_old_ref=$(<"${z_oldref_file}")
   258→
   259→    if test "${z_old_ref}" = "${z_full_ref}"; then
   260→      buc_info "${z_varname}: unchanged"
   261→      z_unchanged=$((z_unchanged + 1))
   262→    else
   263→      buc_info "${z_varname}: ${z_old_ref} -> ${z_full_ref}"
   264→
   265→      sed "s|^${z_varname}=.*|${z_varname}=\"${z_full_ref}\"|" "${z_rbrr_file}" > "${z_sed_value_file}" \
   266→        || buc_die "Failed to sed value for ${z_varname}"
   267→      mv "${z_sed_value_file}" "${z_rbrr_file}" || buc_die "Failed to update ${z_varname} in rbrr file"
   268→
   269→      buc_log_args "Updating vintage comment for ${z_varname}"
   270→      sed "/${z_varname}=/{
   271→        x
   272→        s|(~[^)]*)|(~${z_vintage})|
   273→        x
   274→      }" "${z_rbrr_file}" > "${z_sed_vintage_file}" \
   275→        || buc_die "Failed to update vintage comment for ${z_varname}"
   276→      test -s "${z_sed_vintage_file}" || buc_die "Vintage sed produced empty output for ${z_varname}"
   277→      mv "${z_sed_vintage_file}" "${z_rbrr_file}" || buc_die "Failed to update vintage for ${z_varname}"
   278→
   279→      z_updated=$((z_updated + 1))
   280→    fi
   281→  done
   282→
   283→  buc_step "Refresh complete: ${z_updated} updated, ${z_unchanged} unchanged"
   284→}
   285→
   286→######################################################################
   287→# Main dispatch
   288→
   289→zrbrr_cli_kindle
   290→zrbcc_kindle
   291→
   292→z_command="${1:-}"
   293→
   294→case "${z_command}" in
   295→  validate|render)
   296→    z_file="${RBCC_RBRR_FILE}"
   297→    test -f "${z_file}" || buc_die "RBRR regime file not found: ${z_file}"
   298→    case "${z_command}" in
   299→      validate) rbrr_validate "${z_file}" ;;
   300→      render)   rbrr_render "${z_file}" ;;
   301→    esac
   302→    ;;
   303→  refresh_gcb_pins)
   304→    rbrr_refresh_gcb_pins
   305→    ;;
   306→  *)
   307→    buc_die "Unknown command: ${z_command}. Usage: rbrr_cli.sh {validate|render|refresh_gcb_pins}"
   308→    ;;
   309→esac
   310→
   311→# eof
   312→

1000→=== {gcp_service} Definitions
  1001→
  1002→[[rbtgi_depot_project]]
  1003→// ⟦axl_voices axig_project⟧
  1004→{rbtgi_depot_project}::
  1005→The dedicated
  1006→{gcp_service}
  1007→project instance containing all Recipe Bottle depot resources, APIs, and
  1008→{giam_service_account_s}
  1009→for container building and management. Created and managed by the
  1010→{rbtr_payor}
  1011→role.
  1012→
  1013→[[rbtgi_depot]]
  1014→{rbtgi_depot}::
  1015→A cohesive unit of Recipe Bottle infrastructure comprising a
  1016→{rbtgi_depot_project},
  1017→{rbtgi_build_bucket},
  1018→{rbtgi_repository},
  1019→and
  1020→{rbtr_mason}
  1021→service account that share their productive lifecycle. Created as a unit by
  1022→{rbtgo_depot_create}
  1023→and destroyed as a unit by
  1024→{rbtgo_depot_destroy}.
  1025→
  1026→[[rbtgi_payor_project]]
  1027→// ⟦axl_voices axig_project⟧
  1028→{rbtgi_payor_project}::
  1029→The administrative
  1030→{gcp_service}
  1031→project that hosts the
  1032→{rbtr_payor}
  1033→{giam_service_account}.
  1034→This
  1035→{gcp_project_id}
  1036→serves as the control plane for creating and managing multiple
  1037→{rbtgi_depot_project}
  1038→instances.
  1039→
  1040→
  1041→[[rbtgi_build_bucket]]
  1042→// ⟦axl_voices axig_bucket⟧
  1043→{rbtgi_build_bucket}::
  1044→The dedicated
  1045→{gcs_bucket}
  1046→within the
  1047→{rbtgi_depot_project}
  1048→that holds
  1049→{at_recipe_bottle}
  1050→build artifacts with configured lifecycle policies.
  1051→
  1052→=== {gar_service} Definitions
  1053→
  1054→[[rbtgi_repository]]
  1055→// ⟦axl_voices axig_artifact_repository⟧
  1056→{rbtgi_repository}::
  1057→The managed
  1058→{gar_service}
  1059→repository within the
  1060→{rbtgi_depot_project}
  1061→that stores
  1062→{at_recipe_bottle}
  1063→{rbtgi_image_s}
  1064→and metadata.
  1065→
  1066→[[rbtgi_image]]
  1067→// ⟦axl_voices axig_container_image⟧
  1068→{rbtgi_image}::
  1069→A
  1070→{at_recipe_bottle}
  1071→container image stored in the
  1072→{rbtgi_repository}
  1073→with associated tags and digest.
  1074→
  1075→[[rbtgi_metadata]]
  1076→// ⟦axl_voices axig_generic_artifact⟧
  1077→{rbtgi_metadata}::
  1078→A compressed archive containing
  1079→{at_recipe_bottle}
  1080→build transcript, SBOM, and provenance data stored in the
  1081→{rbtgi_repository}
  1082→as a Generic Artifact.
  1083→
  1084→=== Ark Definitions
  1085→
  1086→[[rbtga_ark]]
  1087→// ⟦axl_voices axig_artifact_group⟧
  1088→{rbtga_ark}::
  1089→The coherent unit of a
  1090→{at_recipe_bottle}
  1091→build output: a paired set of artifacts in the
  1092→{rbtgi_repository}
  1093→sharing a common
  1094→{rbtga_consecration}
  1095→but distinguished by suffix.
  1096→An ark comprises the
  1097→{rbtga_ark_image}
  1098→(`-image` suffix) containing the container image and the
  1099→{rbtga_ark_about}
  1100→(`-about` suffix) containing build provenance and metadata.
  1101→Arks are conjured atomically by
  1102→{rbtgo_ark_conjure}
  1103→and abjured atomically by
  1104→{rbtgo_ark_abjure}.
  1105→
  1106→[[rbtga_consecration]]
  1107→// ⟦axl_voices axtg_timestamp⟧
  1108→{rbtga_consecration}::
  1109→The timestamp-based identifier marking when a vessel was consecrated into an
  1110→{rbtga_ark}.
  1111→Format: `YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ` or similar timestamp pattern.
  1112→Combined with vessel to form complete artifact tags: `«VESSEL»:«CONSECRATION»-image` and `«VESSEL»:«CONSECRATION»-about`.
  1113→
  1114→[[rbtga_ark_image]]
  1115→// ⟦axl_voices axig_container_image⟧
  1116→{rbtga_ark_image}::
  1117→The container image artifact within an
  1118→{rbtga_ark},
  1119→tagged with the `-image` suffix.
  1120→Contains the executable container layers built by
  1121→{gcb_service}.
  1122→
  1123→[[rbtga_ark_about]]
  1124→// ⟦axl_voices axig_generic_artifact⟧
  1125→{rbtga_ark_about}::
  1126→The metadata artifact within an
  1127→{rbtga_ark},
  1128→tagged with the `-about` suffix.
  1129→Contains build provenance, SBOM (Software Bill of Materials), and build transcript assembled during
  1130→{rbtgo_trigger_build}.
  1131→
  1132→=== Support Infrastructure Definitions
  1133→
  1134→[[giam_binding]]
  1135→// ⟦axl_voices axig_binding⟧
  1136→{giam_binding}::
  1137→An association between a
  1138→{giam_service_account}
  1139→and a specific permission role within a resource scope.
  1140→
  1141→[[oauth_token]]
  1142→{oauth_token}::
  1143→A time-limited credential exchanged from
  1144→{giam_service_account}
  1145→keys for API authentication.
  1146→
  1147→
  1148→[[rbtgi_rbra_file]]
  1149→// ⟦axl_voices axig_service_account_key⟧
  1150→{rbtgi_rbra_file}::
  1151→A bash-sourceable environment file containing Google
  1152→{giam_service_account}
  1153→credentials in RBRA (Recipe Bottle Role Authentication) format.
  1154→Created by
  1155→{rbtoe_rbra_generate}
  1156→from
  1157→{rbtgi_json_key_file}
  1158→with field mappings: `RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL` from `.client_email`, `RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY` from `.private_key`, `RBRA_PROJECT_ID` from `.project_id`.
  1159→Loaded via
  1160→{rbtoe_rbra_load}
  1161→with validation of all required fields.
  1162→
  1163→[[rbtgi_json_key_file]]
  1164→// ⟦axl_voices axig_service_account_key⟧
  1165→{rbtgi_json_key_file}::
  1166→A Google
  1167→{giam_service_account}
  1168→JSON key file in its native format as downloaded from the Google Cloud Console, containing the complete credential structure including private_key, client_email, project_id, and other metadata required for authentication.
  1169→
  1170→=== {gcp_service} Core Definitions
  1171→
  1172→[[gcp_organization]]
  1173→// ⟦axl_voices axig_organization⟧
  1174→{gcp_organization}::
  1175→The top-level administrative entity in
  1176→{gcp_service}
  1177→that contains projects and folders.
  1178→
  1179→[[gcp_folder]]
  1180→// ⟦axl_voices axig_folder⟧
  1181→{gcp_folder}::
  1182→An organizational unit within a
  1183→{gcp_organization}
  1184→that groups
  1185→{gcp_project_id_s}
  1186→for management purposes.
  1187→
  1188→[[gcp_project_number]]
  1189→// ⟦axl_voices axtg_project_number⟧
  1190→{gcp_project_number}::
  1191→A globally unique numeric identifier automatically assigned to every
  1192→{gcp_service}
  1193→{gcp_project_id}.
  1194→
  1195→[[gcp_project_id]]
  1196→// ⟦axl_voices axtg_project_id⟧
  1197→{gcp_project_id}::
  1198→A globally unique string identifier chosen during
  1199→{gcp_project_id}
  1200→creation that serves as the
  1201→{gcp_project_id}
  1202→'s primary reference.
  1203→
  1204→[[gcp_lro]]
  1205→// ⟦axl_voices axig_lro⟧
  1206→{gcp_lro}::
  1207→A Long Running Operation, an asynchronous operation in
  1208→{gcp_service}
  1209→that returns immediately but continues processing in the background.
  1210→
  1211→[[gcp_delete_requested]]
  1212→// ⟦axl_voices axig_project_state⟧
  1213→{gcp_delete_requested}::
  1214→A
  1215→{gcp_project_id}
  1216→lifecycle state indicating deletion has been initiated but the
  1217→{gcp_project_id}
  1218→remains recoverable for 30 days.
  1219→
  1220→[[gcp_billing_enabled]]
  1221→// ⟦axl_voices axig_project_state⟧
  1222→{gcp_billing_enabled}::
  1223→A
  1224→{gcp_project_id}
  1225→status indicating that billing is active and the
  1226→{gcp_project_id}
  1227→can incur charges.
  1228→
  1229→=== Google Service Definitions
  1230→
  1231→[[gar_registry]]
  1232→// ⟦axl_voices axig_artifact_registry⟧
  1233→{gar_registry}::
  1234→Google's managed service for storing and managing container images and other software artifacts.
  1235→
  1236→[[gcs_bucket]]
  1237→// ⟦axl_voices axig_bucket⟧
  1238→{gcs_bucket}::
  1239→A container for storing objects in
  1240→{gcs_service}
  1241→with configurable access controls and lifecycle policies.
  1242→
  1243→[[gcb_build]]
  1244→// ⟦axl_voices axig_build⟧
  1245→{gcb_build}::
  1246→An execution instance of
  1247→{gcb_service}
  1248→that processes build instructions to create artifacts.
  1249→
  1250→[[giam_role]]
  1251→// ⟦axl_voices axig_role⟧
  1252→{giam_role}::
  1253→A collection of permissions that can be granted to users,
  1254→{giam_service_account_s},
  1255→or groups in
  1256→{gcp_service}.
  1257→
  1258→[[gcp_lien]]
  1259→// ⟦axl_voices axig_lien⟧
  1260→{gcp_lien}::
  1261→A restriction placed on a
  1262→{gcp_service}
  1263→resource to prevent accidental deletion or modification.
  1264→
  1265→[[giam_service_account]]
  1266→// ⟦axl_voices axig_service_account⟧
  1267→{giam_service_account}::
  1268→A
  1269→{gcp_service}
  1270→identity that applications and services use to authenticate and authorize API operations, consisting of an email address, unique ID, and associated cryptographic keys for non-interactive authentication.
  1271→
  1272→=== Cross-Reference Definitions
  1273→
  1274→[[at_rbm_system]]
  1275→{at_rbm_system}::
  1276→The complete
  1277→{at_recipe_bottle}
  1278→system enabling secure container management.
  1279→
  1280→[[at_bottle_image]]
  1281→// ⟦axl_voices axig_container_image⟧
  1282→{at_bottle_image}::
  1283→A container image built or replicated through the Google build infrastructure.
  1284→
  1285→[[rbtr_consumer]]
  1286→// ⟦axl_voices axo_actor⟧
  1287→{rbtr_consumer}::
  1288→An
  1289→{rbtr_role}
  1290→that an
  1291→{at_operator}
  1292→assumes to use deployed
  1293→{at_bottle_image_s}
  1294→on their
  1295→{at_workstation}.
  1296→The
  1297→{rbtr_consumer}
  1298→interacts with bottle and sentry containers to accomplish work, distinct from roles that build and deploy the system.
  1299→
  1300→[[at_operator]]
  1301→// ⟦axl_voices axo_actor⟧
  1302→{at_operator}::
  1303→An individual who maintains a local installation of the
  1304→{at_recipe_bottle}
  1305→system on their
  1306→{at_workstation}
  1307→and holds
  1308→{rbtgi_rbra_file}
  1309→credentials that enable assumption of the system's defined
  1310→{rbtr_role_s}.
  1311→
  1312→[[at_workstation]]
  1313→{at_workstation}::
  1314→The local environment where
  1315→{at_operator_s}
  1316→execute
  1317→{at_rbm_system}
  1318→commands.
  1319→
  1320→[[at_base_utilities]]
  1321→// ⟦axl_voices axo_dependency⟧
  1322→{at_base_utilities}::
  1323→The minimal set of command-line tools
  1324→(`bash`, `git`, `curl`, `openssh`, `jq`)
  1325→required on the
  1326→{at_workstation}.
  1327→
  1328→[[at_build_service]]
  1329→{at_build_service}::
  1330→A system managing container creation through Google Cloud Build and Google Artifact Registry,
  1331→enabling
  1332→{at_operator_s}
  1333→to build containers in isolated environments with complete traceability
  1334→and cryptographic attestation.
  1335→
  1336→[[at_runtime]]
  1337→{at_runtime}::
  1338→Application capable of running
  1339→{st_container_s}
  1340→on the
  1341→{at_workstation}.
  1342→At the time of this writing, `podman` provides the necessary features for full
  1343→{at_bottle_service}
  1344→provisions.
  1345→
  1346→=== Local Architecture Definitions
  1347→
  1348→[[at_sessile_service]]
  1349→{at_sessile_service}::
  1350→A long-running
  1351→{at_bottle_service}
  1352→that starts via
  1353→{opbs_bottle_start}
  1354→and persists until explicitly stopped.
  1355→The sessile pattern is used for services that maintain state and provide ongoing functionality.
  1356→
  1357→[[at_agile_service]]
  1358→{at_agile_service}::
  1359→A transient
  1360→{at_bottle_service}
  1361→that executes via
  1362→{opbr_bottle_run}
  1363→and terminates when its command completes.
  1364→The agile pattern is used for one-shot operations that produce output and exit.
  1365→
  1366→[[at_bottle_service]]
  1367→{at_bottle_service}::
  1368→A complete service instance consisting of one
  1369→{at_sentry_container},
  1370→one
  1371→{at_censer_container},
  1372→and one
  1373→{at_bottle_container}
  1374→working together to provide secure functionality.
  1375→Each service maintains strict file system and network boundaries while enabling controlled access to resources.
  1376→
  1377→[[at_sentry_container]]
  1378→{at_sentry_container}::
  1379→A privileged container that enforces network security policies for a
  1380→{at_bottle_service}.
  1381→It connects to both
  1382→{at_transit_network}
  1383→and
  1384→{at_enclave_network},
  1385→controlling all network traffic and providing DNS services.
  1386→
  1387→[[at_censer_container]]
  1388→{at_censer_container}::
  1389→A privileged container using a minimal image that establishes and configures the network namespace before the
  1390→{at_bottle_container}
  1391→starts.
  1392→It sets up routing to ensure all traffic flows through the
  1393→{at_sentry_container},
  1394→then shares this pre-configured namespace with the
  1395→{at_bottle_container}
  1396→via `--net=container:censer`.
  1397→This ensures security policies are enforced from the first packet.
  1398→
  1399→[[at_bottle_container]]
  1400→{at_bottle_container}::
  1401→A container that runs the actual service functionality, inheriting its network namespace from the
  1402→{at_censer_container}.
  1403→It is connected only to the
  1404→{at_enclave_network}
  1405→and configured with specific
  1406→{st_volume_mount_s}.
  1407→All external communication flows through its associated
  1408→{at_sentry_container}.
  1409→
  1410→[[at_enclave_network]]
  1411→{at_enclave_network}::
  1412→An isolated network connecting a
  1413→{at_bottle_container}
  1414→to its
  1415→{at_sentry_container},
  1416→using
  1417→{rbrn_enclave_base_ip}/{rbrn_enclave_netmask}
  1418→for addressing.
  1419→This network enforces strict security policies and provides the only network communication path for the
  1420→{at_bottle_container}.
  1421→
  1422→[[at_transit_network]]
  1423→{at_transit_network}::
  1424→The network interface that connects a
  1425→{at_sentry_container}
  1426→to the
  1427→{at_workstation_p}
  1428→network resources.
  1429→This network enables controlled external communication while maintaining security boundaries.
  1430→
  1431→[[at_nameplate]]
  1432→{at_nameplate}::
  1433→A configuration file that defines all aspects of a
  1434→{at_bottle_service},
  1435→including its images, network settings, and security policies.
  1436→Each nameplate provides a complete service specification that the
  1437→{at_rbm_system}
  1438→can instantiate.
  1439→
  1440→[[at_ebpf_program]]
  1441→{at_ebpf_program}::
  1442→An eBPF TC (Traffic Control) filter program attached to the
  1443→{at_bottle_container_p}
  1444→veth interface that intercepts and rewrites Ethernet frames at the packet level.
  1445→This program ensures all
  1446→{at_bottle_container}
  1447→network traffic routes through its associated
  1448→{at_sentry_container}
  1449→without requiring network namespace manipulation or special container privileges.
  1450→
  1451→[[st_container]]
  1452→{st_container}::
  1453→A lightweight, standalone executable package containing all necessary components to run a service.
  1454→
  1455→[[st_volume_mount]]
  1456→{st_volume_mount}::
  1457→A configuration that allows
  1458→{st_container_s}
  1459→to access specified portions of the host filesystem.
  1460→
  1461→[[st_port_map]]
  1462→{st_port_map}::
  1463→A specification of how network ports should be exposed and mapped between
  1464→{st_container_s}
  1465→and the host system.
  1466→
  1467→[[at_sentry_image]]
  1468→{at_sentry_image}::
  1469→A
  1470→{st_image}
  1471→that contains the security enforcement functionality for a
  1472→{at_sentry_container}.
  1473→
  1474→[[at_moniker]]
  1475→{at_moniker}::
  1476→A unique identifier that distinguishes each
  1477→{at_bottle_service}
  1478→instance within the
  1479→{at_rbm_system}.
  1480→This identifier forms the basis for container names and network identifiers.
  1481→
  1482→[[at_rbm_console]]
  1483→{at_rbm_console}::
  1484→The primary operational automation that implements the
  1485→{at_rbm_system}
  1486→lifecycle rules for managing
  1487→{at_bottle_service_s}.
  1488→
  1489→[[at_rbm_config]]
  1490→{at_rbm_config}::
  1491→Configuration settings for the
  1492→{at_rbm_system},
  1493→including
  1494→{at_rbm_secret_s}
  1495→and environment-specific parameters.
  1496→This file typically remains outside version control to protect sensitive data.
  1497→
  1498→[[at_rbm_secret]]
  1499→{at_rbm_secret}::

1600→
  1601→== Configuration Regimes
  1602→
  1603→[[rbrr_prefix]]

  1617→
  1618→[[rbrv_prefix]]
